translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: In the beginning of God's preparing the heavens and the earth --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: the earth hath existed waste and void, and darkness [is] on the face of the deep, and the Spirit of God fluttering on the face of the waters,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and God saith, 'Let light be;' and light is.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And God seeth the light that [it is] good, and God separateth between the light and the darkness,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and God calleth to the light 'Day,' and to the darkness He hath called 'Night;' and there is an evening, and there is a morning -- day one.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And God saith, 'Let an expanse be in the midst of the waters, and let it be separating between waters and waters.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And God maketh the expanse, and it separateth between the waters which [are] under the expanse, and the waters which [are] above the expanse: and it is so.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And God calleth to the expanse 'Heavens;' and there is an evening, and there is a morning -- day second.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And God saith, 'Let the waters under the heavens be collected unto one place, and let the dry land be seen:' and it is so.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And God calleth to the dry land 'Earth,' and to the collection of the waters He hath called 'Seas;' and God seeth that [it is] good.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And God saith, 'Let the earth yield tender grass, herb sowing seed, fruit-tree (whose seed [is] in itself) making fruit after its kind, on the earth:' and it is so.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the earth bringeth forth tender grass, herb sowing seed after its kind, and tree making fruit (whose seed [is] in itself) after its kind; and God seeth that [it is] good;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and there is an evening, and there is a morning -- day third.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And God saith, 'Let luminaries be in the expanse of the heavens, to make a separation between the day and the night, then they have been for signs, and for seasons, and for days and years,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they have been for luminaries in the expanse of the heavens to give light upon the earth:' and it is so.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And God maketh the two great luminaries, the great luminary for the rule of the day, and the small luminary -- and the stars -- for the rule of the night;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and God giveth them in the expanse of the heavens to give light upon the earth,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and to rule over day and over night, and to make a separation between the light and the darkness; and God seeth that [it is] good;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and there is an evening, and there is a morning -- day fourth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And God saith, 'Let the waters teem with the teeming living creature, and fowl let fly on the earth on the face of the expanse of the heavens.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And God prepareth the great monsters, and every living creature that is creeping, which the waters have teemed with, after their kind, and every fowl with wing, after its kind, and God seeth that [it is] good.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And God blesseth them, saying, 'Be fruitful, and multiply, and fill the waters in the seas, and the fowl let multiply in the earth:'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and there is an evening, and there is a morning -- day fifth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And God saith, 'Let the earth bring forth the living creature after its kind, cattle and creeping thing, and beast of the earth after its kind:' and it is so.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And God maketh the beast of the earth after its kind, and the cattle after their kind, and every creeping thing of the ground after its kind, and God seeth that [it is] good.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And God saith, 'Let Us make man in Our image, according to Our likeness, and let them rule over fish of the sea, and over fowl of the heavens, and over cattle, and over all the earth, and over every creeping thing that is creeping on the earth.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And God prepareth the man in His image; in the image of God He prepared him, a male and a female He prepared them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And God blesseth them, and God saith to them, 'Be fruitful, and multiply, and fill the earth, and subdue it, and rule over fish of the sea, and over fowl of the heavens, and over every living thing that is creeping upon the earth.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And God saith, 'Lo, I have given to you every herb sowing seed, which [is] upon the face of all the earth, and every tree in which [is] the fruit of a tree sowing seed, to you it is for food;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and to every beast of the earth, and to every fowl of the heavens, and to every creeping thing on the earth, in which [is] breath of life, every green herb [is] for food:' and it is so.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And God seeth all that He hath done, and lo, very good; and there is an evening, and there is a morning -- day the sixth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the heavens and the earth are completed, and all their host;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and God completeth by the seventh day His work which He hath made, and ceaseth by the seventh day from all His work which He hath made.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And God blesseth the seventh day, and sanctifieth it, for in it He hath ceased from all His work which God had prepared for making.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: These [are] births of the heavens and of the earth in their being prepared, in the day of Jehovah God's making earth and heavens;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and no shrub of the field is yet in the earth, and no herb of the field yet sprouteth, for Jehovah God hath not rained upon the earth, and a man there is not to serve the ground,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and a mist goeth up from the earth, and hath watered the whole face of the ground.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah God formeth the man -- dust from the ground, and breatheth into his nostrils breath of life, and the man becometh a living creature.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah God planteth a garden in Eden, at the east, and He setteth there the man whom He hath formed;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jehovah God causeth to sprout from the ground every tree desirable for appearance, and good for food, and the tree of life in the midst of the garden, and the tree of knowledge of good and evil.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And a river is going out from Eden to water the garden, and from thence it is parted, and hath become four chief [rivers];

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: the name of the one [is] Pison, it [is] that which is surrounding the whole land of the Havilah where the gold [is],

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the gold of that land [is] good, there [is] the bdolach and the shoham stone;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the name of the second river [is] Gibon, it [is] that which is surrounding the whole land of Cush;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the name of the third river [is] Hiddekel, it [is] that which is going east of Asshur; and the fourth river is Phrat.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah God taketh the man, and causeth him to rest in the garden of Eden, to serve it, and to keep it.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah God layeth a charge on the man, saying, 'Of every tree of the garden eating thou dost eat;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and of the tree of knowledge of good and evil, thou dost not eat of it, for in the day of thine eating of it -- dying thou dost die.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah God saith, 'Not good for the man to be alone, I do make to him an helper -- as his counterpart.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah God formeth from the ground every beast of the field, and every fowl of the heavens, and bringeth in unto the man, to see what he doth call it; and whatever the man calleth a living creature, that [is] its name.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the man calleth names to all the cattle, and to fowl of the heavens, and to every beast of the field; and to man hath not been found an helper -- as his counterpart.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah God causeth a deep sleep to fall upon the man, and he sleepeth, and He taketh one of his ribs, and closeth up flesh in its stead.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah God buildeth up the rib which He hath taken out of the man into a woman, and bringeth her in unto the man;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the man saith, 'This [is] the [proper] step! bone of my bone, and flesh of my flesh!' for this it is called Woman, for from a man hath this been taken;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: therefore doth a man leave his father and his mother, and hath cleaved unto his wife, and they have become one flesh.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they are both of them naked, the man and his wife, and they are not ashamed of themselves.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the serpent hath been subtile above every beast of the field which Jehovah God hath made, and he saith unto the woman, 'Is it true that God hath said, Ye do not eat of every tree of the garden?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the woman saith unto the serpent, 'Of the fruit of the trees of the garden we do eat,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and of the fruit of the tree which [is] in the midst of the garden God hath said, Ye do not eat of it, nor touch it, lest ye die.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the serpent saith unto the woman, 'Dying, ye do not die,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for God doth know that in the day of your eating of it -- your eyes have been opened, and ye have been as God, knowing good and evil.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the woman seeth that the tree [is] good for food, and that it [is] pleasant to the eyes, and the tree is desirable to make [one] wise, and she taketh of its fruit and eateth, and giveth also to her husband with her, and he doth eat;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the eyes of them both are opened, and they know that they [are] naked, and they sew fig-leaves, and make to themselves girdles.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they hear the sound of Jehovah God walking up and down in the garden at the breeze of the day, and the man and his wife hide themselves from the face of Jehovah God in the midst of the trees of the garden.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah God calleth unto the man, and saith to him, 'Where [art] thou?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he saith, 'Thy sound I have heard in the garden, and I am afraid, for I am naked, and I hide myself.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And He saith, 'Who hath declared to thee that thou [art] naked? of the tree of which I have commanded thee not to eat, hast thou eaten?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the man saith, 'The woman whom Thou didst place with me -- she hath given to me of the tree -- and I do eat.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah God saith to the woman, 'What [is] this thou hast done?' and the woman saith, 'The serpent hath caused me to forget -- and I do eat.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah God saith unto the serpent, 'Because thou hast done this, cursed [art] thou above all the cattle, and above every beast of the field: on thy belly dost thou go, and dust thou dost eat, all days of thy life;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and enmity I put between thee and the woman, and between thy seed and her seed; he doth bruise thee -- the head, and thou dost bruise him -- the heel.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Unto the woman He said, 'Multiplying I multiply thy sorrow and thy conception, in sorrow dost thou bear children, and toward thy husband [is] thy desire, and he doth rule over thee.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And to the man He said, 'Because thou hast hearkened to the voice of thy wife, and dost eat of the tree concerning which I have charged thee, saying, Thou dost not eat of it, cursed [is] the ground on thine account; in sorrow thou dost eat of it all days of thy life,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and thorn and bramble it doth bring forth to thee, and thou hast eaten the herb of the field;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: by the sweat of thy face thou dost eat bread till thy return unto the ground, for out of it hast thou been taken, for dust thou [art], and unto dust thou turnest back.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the man calleth his wife's name Eve: for she hath been mother of all living.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah God doth make to the man and to his wife coats of skin, and doth clothe them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah God saith, 'Lo, the man was as one of Us, as to the knowledge of good and evil; and now, lest he send forth his hand, and have taken also of the tree of life, and eaten, and lived to the age,' --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Jehovah God sendeth him forth from the garden of Eden to serve the ground from which he hath been taken;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: yea, he casteth out the man, and causeth to dwell at the east of the garden of Eden the cherubs and the flame of the sword which is turning itself round to guard the way of the tree of life.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the man knew Eve his wife, and she conceiveth and beareth Cain, and saith, 'I have gotten a man by Jehovah;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and she addeth to bear his brother, even Abel. And Abel is feeding a flock, and Cain hath been servant of the ground.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it cometh to pass at the end of days that Cain bringeth from the fruit of the ground a present to Jehovah;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Abel, he hath brought, he also, from the female firstlings of his flock, even from their fat ones; and Jehovah looketh unto Abel and unto his present,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and unto Cain and unto his present He hath not looked; and it is very displeasing to Cain, and his countenance is fallen.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah saith unto Cain, 'Why hast thou displeasure? and why hath thy countenance fallen?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Is there not, if thou dost well, acceptance? and if thou dost not well, at the opening a sin-offering is crouching, and unto thee its desire, and thou rulest over it.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Cain saith unto Abel his brother, ['Let us go into the field;'] and it cometh to pass in their being in the field, that Cain riseth up against Abel his brother, and slayeth him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah saith unto Cain, 'Where [is] Abel thy brother?' and he saith, 'I have not known; my brother's keeper -- I?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And He saith, 'What hast thou done? the voice of thy brother's blood is crying unto Me from the ground;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and now, cursed [art] thou from the ground, which hath opened her mouth to receive the blood of thy brother from thy hand;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: when thou tillest the ground, it doth not add to give its strength to thee -- a wanderer, even a trembling one, thou art in the earth.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Cain saith unto Jehovah, 'Greater is my punishment than to be borne;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: lo, Thou hast driven me to-day from off the face of the ground, and from Thy face I am hid; and I have been a wanderer, even a trembling one, in the earth, and it hath been -- every one finding me doth slay me.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah saith to him, 'Therefore -- of any slayer of Cain sevenfold it is required;' and Jehovah setteth to Cain a token that none finding him doth slay him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Cain goeth out from before Jehovah, and dwelleth in the land, moving about east of Eden;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Cain knoweth his wife, and she conceiveth, and beareth Enoch; and he is building a city, and he calleth the name of the city, according to the name of his son -- Enoch.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And born to Enoch is Irad; and Irad hath begotten Mehujael; and Mehujael hath begotten Methusael; and Methusael hath begotten Lamech.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Lamech taketh to himself two wives, the name of the one Adah, and the name of the second Zillah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Adah beareth Jabal, he hath been father of those inhabiting tents and purchased possessions;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the name of his brother [is] Jubal, he hath been father of every one handling harp and organ.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Zillah she also bare Tubal-Cain, an instructor of every artificer in brass and iron; and a sister of Tubal-Cain [is] Naamah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Lamech saith to his wives: -- 'Adah and Zillah, hear my voice; Wives of Lamech, give ear [to] my saying: For a man I have slain for my wound, Even a young man for my hurt;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For sevenfold is required for Cain, And for Lamech seventy and sevenfold.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Adam again knoweth his wife, and she beareth a son, and calleth his name Seth, 'for God hath appointed for me another seed instead of Abel:' for Cain had slain him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And to Seth, to him also a son hath been born, and he calleth his name Enos; then a beginning was made of preaching in the name of Jehovah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: This [is] an account of the births of Adam: In the day of God's preparing man, in the likeness of God He hath made him;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: a male and a female He hath prepared them, and He blesseth them, and calleth their name Man, in the day of their being prepared.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Adam liveth an hundred and thirty years, and begetteth [a son] in his likeness, according to his image, and calleth his name Seth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the days of Adam after his begetting Seth are eight hundred years, and he begetteth sons and daughters.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And all the days of Adam which he lived are nine hundred and thirty years, and he dieth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Seth liveth an hundred and five years, and begetteth Enos.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Seth liveth after his begetting Enos eight hundred and seven years, and begetteth sons and daughters.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And all the days of Seth are nine hundred and twelve years, and he dieth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Enos liveth ninety years, and begetteth Cainan.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Enos liveth after his begetting Cainan eight hundred and fifteen years, and begetteth sons and daughters.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And all the days of Enos are nine hundred and five years, and he dieth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Cainan liveth seventy years, and begetteth Mahalaleel.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Cainan liveth after his begetting Mahalaleel eight hundred and forty years, and begetteth sons and daughters.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And all the days of Cainan are nine hundred and ten years, and he dieth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Mahalaleel liveth five and sixty years, and begetteth Jared.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Mahalaleel liveth after his begetting Jared eight hundred and thirty years, and begetteth sons and daughters.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And all the days of Mahalaleel are eight hundred and ninety and five years, and he dieth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jared liveth an hundred and sixty and two years, and begetteth Enoch.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jared liveth after his begetting Enoch eight hundred years, and begetteth sons and daughters.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And all the days of Jared are nine hundred and sixty and two years, and he dieth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Enoch liveth five and sixty years, and begetteth Methuselah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Enoch walketh habitually with God after his begetting Methuselah three hundred years, and begetteth sons and daughters.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And all the days of Enoch are three hundred and sixty and five years.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Enoch walketh habitually with God, and he is not, for God hath taken him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Methuselah liveth an hundred and eighty and seven years, and begetteth Lamech.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Methuselah liveth after his begetting Lamech seven hundred and eighty and two years, and begetteth sons and daughters.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And all the days of Methuselah are nine hundred and sixty and nine years, and he dieth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Lamech liveth an hundred and eighty and two years, and begetteth a son,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and calleth his name Noah, saying, 'This [one] doth comfort us concerning our work, and concerning the labour of our hands, because of the ground which Jehovah hath cursed.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Lamech liveth after his begetting Noah five hundred and ninety and five years, and begetteth sons and daughters.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And all the days of Lamech are seven hundred and seventy and seven years, and he dieth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Noah is a son of five hundred years, and Noah begetteth Shem, Ham, and Japheth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it cometh to pass that mankind have begun to multiply on the face of the ground, and daughters have been born to them,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and sons of God see the daughters of men that they [are] fair, and they take to themselves women of all whom they have chosen.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah saith, 'My Spirit doth not strive in man -- to the age; in their erring they [are] flesh:' and his days have been an hundred and twenty years.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The fallen ones were in the earth in those days, and even afterwards when sons of God come in unto daughters of men, and they have borne to them -- they [are] the heroes, who, from of old, [are] the men of name.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah seeth that abundant [is] the wickedness of man in the earth, and every imagination of the thoughts of his heart only evil all the day;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jehovah repenteth that He hath made man in the earth, and He grieveth Himself -- unto His heart.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah saith, 'I wipe away man whom I have prepared from off the face of the ground, from man unto beast, unto creeping thing, and unto fowl of the heavens, for I have repented that I have made them.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Noah found grace in the eyes of Jehovah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: These [are] births of Noah: Noah [is] a righteous man; perfect he hath been among his generations; with God hath Noah walked habitually.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Noah begetteth three sons, Shem, Ham, and Japheth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the earth is corrupt before God, and the earth is filled [with] violence.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And God seeth the earth, and lo, it hath been corrupted, for all flesh hath corrupted its way on the earth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And God said to Noah, 'An end of all flesh hath come before Me, for the earth hath been full of violence from their presence; and lo, I am destroying them with the earth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Make for thyself an ark of gopher-wood; rooms dost thou make with the ark, and thou hast covered it within and without with cypress;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and this [is] that which thou dost with it: three hundred cubits [is] the length of the ark, fifty cubits its breadth, and thirty cubits its height;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: a window dost thou make for the ark, and unto a cubit thou dost restrain it from above; and the opening of the ark thou dost put in its side, -- lower, second, and third [stories] dost thou make it.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And I, lo, I am bringing in the deluge of waters on the earth to destroy all flesh, in which [is] a living spirit, from under the heavens; all that [is] in the earth doth expire.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And I have established My covenant with thee, and thou hast come in unto the ark, thou, and thy sons, and thy wife, and thy son's wives with thee;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and of all that liveth, of all flesh, two of every [sort] thou dost bring in unto the ark, to keep alive with thee; male and female are they.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Of the fowl after its kind, and of the cattle after their kind, of every creeping thing of the ground after its kind, two of every [sort] they come in unto thee, to keep alive.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And thou, take to thyself of all food that is eaten; and thou hast gathered unto thyself, and it hath been to thee and to them for food.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Noah doth according to all that God hath commanded him; so hath he done.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah saith to Noah, 'Come in, thou and all thy house, unto the ark, for thee I have seen righteous before Me in this generation;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: of all the clean beasts thou dost take to thee seven pairs, a male and its female; and of the beasts which are not clean two, a male and its female;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: also, of fowl of the heavens seven pairs, a male and a female, to keep alive seed on the face of all the earth;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for after other seven days I am sending rain on the earth forty days and forty nights, and have wiped away all the substance that I have made from off the face of the ground.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Noah doth according to all that Jehovah hath commanded him:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Noah [is] a son of six hundred years, and the deluge of waters hath been upon the earth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Noah goeth in, and his sons, and his wife, and his sons' wives with him, unto the ark, from the presence of the waters of the deluge;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: of the clean beasts and of the beasts that [are] not clean, and of the fowl, and of every thing that is creeping upon the ground,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: two by two they have come in unto Noah, unto the ark, a male and a female, as God hath commanded Noah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it cometh to pass, after the seventh of the days, that waters of the deluge have been on the earth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: In the six hundredth year of the life of Noah, in the second month, in the seventeenth day of the month, in this day have been broken up all fountains of the great deep, and the net-work of the heavens hath been opened,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the shower is on the earth forty days and forty nights.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: In this self-same day went in Noah, and Shem, and Ham, and Japheth, sons of Noah, and Noah's wife and the three wives of his sons with them, unto the ark;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: they, and every living creature after its kind, and every beast after its kind, and every creeping thing that is creeping on the earth after its kind, and every fowl after its kind, every bird -- every wing.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they come in unto Noah, unto the ark, two by two of all the flesh in which [is] a living spirit;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they that are coming in, male and female of all flesh, have come in as God hath commanded him, and Jehovah doth close [it] for him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the deluge is forty days on the earth, and the waters multiply, and lift up the ark, and it is raised up from off the earth;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the waters are mighty, and multiply exceedingly upon the earth; and the ark goeth on the face of the waters.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the waters have been very very mighty on the earth, and covered are all the high mountains which [are] under the whole heavens;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: fifteen cubits upwards have the waters become mighty, and the mountains are covered;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and expire doth all flesh that is moving on the earth, among fowl, and among cattle, and among beasts, and among all the teeming things which are teeming on the earth, and all mankind;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: all in whose nostrils [is] breath of a living spirit -- of all that [is] in the dry land -- have died.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And wiped away is all the substance that is on the face of the ground, from man unto beast, unto creeping thing, and unto fowl of the heavens; yea, they are wiped away from the earth, and only Noah is left, and those who [are] with him in the ark;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the waters are mighty on the earth a hundred and fifty days.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And God remembereth Noah, and every living thing, and all the cattle which [are] with him in the ark, and God causeth a wind to pass over the earth, and the waters subside,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and closed are the fountains of the deep and the net-work of the heavens, and restrained is the shower from the heavens.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And turn back do the waters from off the earth, going on and returning; and the waters are lacking at the end of a hundred and fifty days.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the ark resteth, in the seventh month, in the seventeenth day of the month, on mountains of Ararat;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the waters have been going and becoming lacking till the tenth month; in the tenth [month], on the first of the month, appeared the heads of the mountains.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it cometh to pass, at the end of forty days, that Noah openeth the window of the ark which he made,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he sendeth forth the raven, and it goeth out, going out and turning back till the drying of the waters from off the earth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he sendeth forth the dove from him to see whether the waters have been lightened from off the face of the ground,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the dove hath not found rest for the sole of her foot, and she turneth back unto him, unto the ark, for waters [are] on the face of all the earth, and he putteth out his hand, and taketh her, and bringeth her in unto him, unto the ark.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he stayeth yet other seven days, and addeth to send forth the dove from the ark;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the dove cometh in unto him at even-time, and lo, an olive leaf torn off in her mouth; and Noah knoweth that the waters have been lightened from off the earth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he stayeth yet other seven days, and sendeth forth the dove, and it added not to turn back unto him any more.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it cometh to pass in the six hundredth and first year, in the first [month], in the first of the month, the waters have been dried from off the earth; and Noah turneth aside the covering of the ark, and looketh, and lo, the face of the ground hath been dried.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And in the second month, in the seven and twentieth day of the month, the earth hath become dry.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And God speaketh unto Noah, saying, 'Go out from the ark, thou, and thy wife, and thy sons, and thy sons' wives with thee;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: every living thing that [is] with thee, of all flesh, among fowl, and among cattle, and among every creeping thing which is creeping on the earth, bring out with thee;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they have teemed in the earth, and been fruitful, and have multiplied on the earth.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Noah goeth out, and his sons, and his wife, and his sons' wives with him;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: every beast, every creeping thing, and every fowl; every creeping thing on the earth, after their families, have gone out from the ark.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Noah buildeth an altar to Jehovah, and taketh of every clean beast, and of every clean fowl, and causeth burnt-offerings to ascend on the altar;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jehovah smelleth the sweet fragrance, and Jehovah saith unto His heart, 'I continue not to disesteem any more the ground because of man, though the imagination of the heart of man [is] evil from his youth; and I continue not to smite any more all living, as I have done;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: during all days of the earth, seed-time and harvest, and cold and heat, and summer and winter, and day and night, do not cease.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And God blesseth Noah, and his sons, and saith to them, 'Be fruitful, and multiply, and fill the earth;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and your fear and your dread is on every beast of the earth, and on every fowl of the heavens, on all that creepeth on the ground, and on all fishes of the sea -- into your hand they have been given.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Every creeping thing that is alive, to you it is for food; as the green herb I have given to you the whole;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: only flesh in its life -- its blood -- ye do not eat.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And only your blood for your lives do I require; from the hand of every living thing I require it, and from the hand of man, from the hand of every man's brother I require the life of man;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: whoso sheddeth man's blood, by man is his blood shed: for in the image of God hath He made man.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And ye, be fruitful and multiply, teem in the earth, and multiply in it.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And God speaketh unto Noah, and unto his sons with him, saying,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And I, lo, I am establishing My covenant with you, and with your seed after you,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and with every living creature which [is] with you, among fowl, among cattle, and among every beast of the earth with you, from all who are going out of the ark -- to every beast of the earth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And I have established My covenant with you, and all flesh is not any more cut off by waters of a deluge, and there is not any more a deluge to destroy the earth.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And God saith, 'This is a token of the covenant which I am giving between Me and you, and every living creature that [is] with you, to generations age-during;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: My bow I have given in the cloud, and it hath been for a token of a covenant between Me and the earth;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and it hath come to pass (in My sending a cloud over the earth) that the bow hath been seen in the cloud,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and I have remembered My covenant which is between Me and you, and every living creature among all flesh, and the waters become no more a deluge to destroy all flesh;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the bow hath been in the cloud, and I have seen it -- to remember the covenant age-during between God and every living creature among all flesh which [is] on the earth.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And God saith unto Noah, 'This [is] a token of the covenant which I have established between Me and all flesh that [is] upon the earth.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the sons of Noah who are going out of the ark are Shem, and Ham, and Japheth; and Ham is father of Canaan.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: These three [are] sons of Noah, and from these hath all the earth been overspread.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Noah remaineth a man of the ground, and planteth a vineyard,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and drinketh of the wine, and is drunken, and uncovereth himself in the midst of the tent.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Ham, father of Canaan, seeth the nakedness of his father, and declareth to his two brethren without.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Shem taketh -- Japheth also -- the garment, and they place on the shoulder of them both, and go backward, and cover the nakedness of their father; and their faces [are] backward, and their father's nakedness they have not seen.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Noah awaketh from his wine, and knoweth that which his young son hath done to him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and saith: 'Cursed [is] Canaan, Servant of servants he is to his brethren.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he saith: 'Blessed of Jehovah my God [is] Shem, And Canaan is servant to him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: God doth give beauty to Japheth, And he dwelleth in tents of Shem, And Canaan is servant to him.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Noah liveth after the deluge three hundred and fifty years;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and all the days of Noah are nine hundred and fifty years, and he dieth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And these [are] births of the sons of Noah, Shem, Ham, and Japheth; and born to them are sons after the deluge.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Sons of Japheth [are] Gomer, and Magog, and Madai, and Javan, and Tubal, and Meshech, and Tiras.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And sons of Gomer [are] Ashkenaz, and Riphath, and Togarmah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And sons of Javan [are] Elishah, and Tarshish, Kittim, and Dodanim.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: By these have the isles of the nations been parted in their lands, each by his tongue, by their families, in their nations.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And sons of Ham [are] Cush, and Mitzraim, and Phut, and Canaan.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And sons of Cush [are] Seba, and Havilah, and Sabtah, and Raamah, and Sabtechah; and sons of Raamah [are] Sheba and Dedan.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Cush hath begotten Nimrod;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: he hath begun to be a hero in the land; he hath been a hero in hunting before Jehovah; therefore it is said, 'As Nimrod the hero [in] hunting before Jehovah.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the first part of his kingdom is Babel, and Erech, and Accad, and Calneh, in the land of Shinar;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: from that land he hath gone out to Asshur, and buildeth Nineveh, even the broad places of the city, and Calah,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Resen, between Nineveh and Calah; it [is] the great city.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Mitzraim hath begotten the Ludim, and the Anamim, and the Lehabim, and the Naphtuhim,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the Pathrusim, and the Casluhim, (whence have come out Philistim,) and the Caphtorim.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Canaan hath begotten Sidon his first-born, and Heth,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the Jebusite, and the Amorite, and the Girgashite,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the Hivite, and the Arkite, and the Sinite,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the Arvadite, and the Zemarite, and the Hamathite; and afterwards have the families of the Canaanite been scattered.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the border of the Canaanite is from Sidon, [in] thy coming towards Gerar, unto Gaza; [in] thy coming towards Sodom, and Gomorrah, and Admah, and Zeboim, unto Lasha.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: These [are] sons of Ham, by their families, by their tongues, in their lands, in their nations.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: As to Shem, father of all sons of Eber, brother of Japheth the elder, he hath also begotten:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Sons of Shem [are] Elam, and Asshur, and Arphaxad, and Lud, and Aram.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And sons of Aram [are] Uz, and Hul, and Gether, and Mash.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Arphaxad hath begotten Salah, and Salah hath begotten Eber.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And to Eber have two sons been born; the name of the one [is] Peleg (for in his days hath the earth been divided,) and his brother's name [is] Joktan.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Joktan hath begotten Almodad, and Sheleph, and Hazarmaveth, and Jerah,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Hadoram, and Uzal, and Diklah,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Obal, and Abimael, and Sheba,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Ophir, and Havilah, and Jobab; all these [are] sons of Joktan;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and their dwelling is from Mesha, [in] thy coming towards Sephar, a mount of the east.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: These [are] sons of Shem, by their families, by their tongues, in their lands, by their nations.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: These [are] families of the sons of Noah, by their births, in their nations, and by these have the nations been parted in the earth after the deluge.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the whole earth is of one pronunciation, and of the same words,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and it cometh to pass, in their journeying from the east, that they find a valley in the land of Shinar, and dwell there;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they say each one to his neighbour, 'Give help, let us make bricks, and burn [them] thoroughly:' and the brick is to them for stone, and the bitumen hath been to them for mortar.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they say, 'Give help, let us build for ourselves a city and tower, and its head in the heavens, and make for ourselves a name, lest we be scattered over the face of all the earth.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah cometh down to see the city and the tower which the sons of men have builded;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jehovah saith, 'Lo, the people [is] one, and one pronunciation [is] to them all, and this it hath dreamed of doing; and now, nothing is restrained from them of that which they have purposed to do.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Give help, let us go down, and mingle there their pronunciation, so that a man doth not understand the pronunciation of his companion.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah doth scatter them from thence over the face of all the earth, and they cease to build the city;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: therefore hath [one] called its name Babel, for there hath Jehovah mingled the pronunciation of all the earth, and from thence hath Jehovah scattered them over the face of all the earth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: These [are] births of Shem: Shem [is] a son of an hundred years, and begetteth Arphaxad two years after the deluge.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Shem liveth after his begetting Arphaxad five hundred years, and begetteth sons and daughters.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Arphaxad hath lived five and thirty years, and begetteth Salah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Arphaxad liveth after his begetting Salah four hundred and three years, and begetteth sons and daughters.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Salah hath lived thirty years, and begetteth Eber.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Salah liveth after his begetting Eber four hundred and three years, and begetteth sons and daughters.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Eber liveth four and thirty years, and begetteth Peleg.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Eber liveth after his begetting Peleg four hundred and thirty years, and begetteth sons and daughters.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Peleg liveth thirty years, and begetteth Reu.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Peleg liveth after his begetting Reu two hundred and nine years, and begetteth sons and daughters.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Reu liveth two and thirty years, and begetteth Serug.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Reu liveth after his begetting Serug two hundred and seven years, and begetteth sons and daughters.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Serug liveth thirty years, and begetteth Nahor.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Serug liveth after his begetting Nahor two hundred years, and begetteth sons and daughters.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Nahor liveth nine and twenty years, and begetteth Terah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Nahor liveth after his begetting Terah an hundred and nineteen years, and begetteth sons and daughters.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Terah liveth seventy years, and begetteth Abram, Nahor, and Haran.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And these [are] births of Terah: Terah hath begotten Abram, Nahor, and Haran; and Haran hath begotten Lot;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Haran dieth in the presence of Terah his father, in the land of his birth, in Ur of the Chaldees.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Abram and Nahor take to themselves wives; the name of Abram's wife [is] Sarai, and the name of Nahor's wife [is] Milcah, daughter of Haran, father of Milcah, and father of Iscah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Sarai is barren -- she hath no child.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Terah taketh Abram his son, and Lot, son of Haran, his son's son, and Sarai his daughter-in-law, wife of Abram his son, and they go out with them from Ur of the Chaldees, to go towards the land of Canaan; and they come unto Charan, and dwell there.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the days of Terah are two hundred and five years, and Terah dieth in Charan.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah saith unto Abram, 'Go for thyself, from thy land, and from thy kindred, and from the house of thy father, unto the land which I shew thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And I make thee become a great nation, and bless thee, and make thy name great; and be thou a blessing.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And I bless those blessing thee, and him who is disesteeming thee I curse, and blessed in thee have been all families of the ground.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Abram goeth on, as Jehovah hath spoken unto him, and Lot goeth with him, and Abram [is] a son of five and seventy years in his going out from Charan.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Abram taketh Sarai his wife, and Lot his brother's son, and all their substance that they have gained, and the persons that they have obtained in Charan; and they go out to go towards the land of Canaan; and they come in to the land of Canaan.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Abram passeth over into the land, unto the place Shechem, unto the oak of Moreh; and the Canaanite [is] then in the land.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah appeareth unto Abram, and saith, 'To thy seed I give this land;' and he buildeth there an altar to Jehovah, who hath appeared unto him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he removeth from thence towards a mountain at the east of Beth-El, and stretcheth out the tent (Beth-El at the west, and Hai at the east), and he buildeth there an altar to Jehovah, and preacheth in the name of Jehovah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Abram journeyeth, going on and journeying towards the south.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And there is a famine in the land, and Abram goeth down towards Egypt to sojourn there, for the famine [is] grievous in the land;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and it cometh to pass as he hath drawn near to enter Egypt, that he saith unto Sarai his wife, 'Lo, I pray thee, I have known that thou [art] a woman of beautiful appearance;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and it hath come to pass that the Egyptians see thee, and they have said, 'This [is] his wife,' and they have slain me, and thee they keep alive:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: say, I pray thee, thou [art] my sister, so that it is well with me because of thee, and my soul hath lived for thy sake.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it cometh to pass, at the entering of Abram into Egypt, that the Egyptians see the woman that she [is] exceeding fair;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and princes of Pharaoh see her, and praise her unto Pharaoh, and the woman is taken [to] Pharaoh's house;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and to Abram he hath done good because of her, and he hath sheep and oxen, and he-asses, and men-servants, and handmaids, and she-asses, and camels.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah plagueth Pharaoh and his house -- great plagues -- for the matter of Sarai, Abram's wife.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Pharaoh calleth for Abram, and saith, 'What [is] this thou hast done to me? why hast thou not declared to me that she [is] thy wife?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Why hast thou said, She [is] my sister, and I take her to myself for a wife? and now, lo, thy wife, take and go.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Pharaoh chargeth men concerning him, and they send him away, and his wife, an all that he hath.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Abram goeth up from Egypt (he and his wife, and all that he hath, and Lot with him) towards the south;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Abram [is] exceedingly wealthy in cattle, in silver, and in gold.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he goeth on his journeyings from the south, even unto Bethel, unto the place where his tent had been at the commencement, between Bethel and Hai --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: unto the place of the altar which he made there at the first, and there doth Abram preach in the name of Jehovah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And also to Lot, who is going with Abram, there hath been sheep and oxen and tents;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the land hath not suffered them to dwell together, for their substance hath been much, and they have not been able to dwell together;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and there is a strife between those feeding Abram's cattle and those feeding Lot's cattle; and the Canaanite and the Perizzite [are] then dwelling in the land.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Abram saith unto Lot, 'Let there not, I pray thee, be strife between me and thee, and between my shepherds and thy shepherds, for we [are] men -- brethren.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Is not all the land before thee? be parted, I pray thee, from me; if to the left, then I to the right; and if to the right, then I to the left.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Lot lifteth up his eyes, and seeth the whole circuit of the Jordan that it [is] all a watered country (before Jehovah's destroying Sodom and Gomorrah, as Jehovah's garden, as the land of Egypt,) in thy coming toward Zoar,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Lot chooseth for himself the whole circuit of the Jordan; and Lot journeyeth from the east, and they are parted -- a man from his companion;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Abram hath dwelt in the land of Canaan, and Lot hath dwelt in the cities of the circuit, and tenteth unto Sodom;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the men of Sodom [are] evil, and sinners before Jehovah exceedingly.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah said unto Abram, after Lot's being parted from him, 'Lift up, I pray thee, thine eyes, and look from the place where thou [art], northward, and southward, and eastward, and westward;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for the whole of the land which thou are seeing, to thee I give it, and to thy seed -- to the age.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And I have set thy seed as dust of the earth, so that, if one is able to number the dust of the earth, even thy seed is numbered;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: rise, go up and down through the land, to its length, and to its breadth, for to thee I give it.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Abram tenteth, and cometh, and dwelleth among the oaks of Mamre, which [are] in Hebron, and buildeth there an altar to Jehovah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it cometh to pass in the days of Amraphel king of Shinar, Arioch king of Ellasar, Chedorlaomer king of Elam, and Tidal king of Goyim,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: they have made war with Bera king of Sodom, and with Birsha king of Gomorrah, Shinab king of Admah, and Shemeber king of Zeboim, and the king of Bela, which [is] Zoar.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: All these have been joined together unto the valley of Siddim, which [is] the Salt Sea;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: twelve years they served Chedorlaomer, and the thirteenth year they rebelled.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And in the fourteenth year came Chedorlaomer, and the kings who [are] with him, and they smite the Rephaim in Ashteroth Karnaim, and the Zuzim in Ham, and the Emim in Shaveh Kiriathaim,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the Horites in their mount Seir, unto El-Paran, which [is] by the wilderness;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they turn back and come in unto En-Mishpat, which [is] Kadesh, and smite the whole field of the Amalekite, and also the Amorite who is dwelling in Hazezon-Tamar.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the king of Sodom goeth out, and the king of Gomorrah, and the king of Admah, and the king of Zeboim, and the king of Bela, which [is] Zoar; and they set the battle in array with them in the valley of Siddim,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: with Chedorlaomer king of Elam, and Tidal king of Goyim, and Amraphel king of Shinar, and Arioch king of Ellasar; four kings with the five.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the valley of Siddim [is] full of bitumen-pits; and the kings of Sodom and Gomorrah flee, and fall there, and those left have fled to the mountain.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they take the whole substance of Sodom and Gomorrah, and the whole of their food, and go away;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they take Lot, Abram's brother's son (seeing he is dwelling in Sodom), and his substance, and go away.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And one who is escaping cometh and declareth to Abram the Hebrew, and he is dwelling among the oaks of Mamre the Amorite, brother of Eshcol, and brother of Aner, and they [are] Abram's allies.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Abram heareth that his brother hath been taken captive, and he draweth out his trained domestics, three hundred and eighteen, and pursueth unto Dan.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he divideth himself against them by night, he and his servants, and smiteth them, and pursueth them unto Hobah, which [is] at the left of Damascus;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he bringeth back the whole of the substance, and also Lot his brother and his substance hath he brought back, and also the women and the people.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the king of Sodom goeth out to meet him (after his turning back from the smiting of Chedorlaomer, and of the kings who [are] with him), unto the valley of Shaveh, which [is] the king's valley.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Melchizedek king of Salem hath brought out bread and wine, and he [is] priest of God Most High;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he blesseth him, and saith, 'Blessed [is] Abram to God Most High, possessing heaven and earth;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and blessed [is] God Most High, who hath delivered thine adversaries into thy hand;' and he giveth to him a tenth of all.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the king of Sodom saith unto Abram, 'Give to me the persons, and the substance take to thyself,'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Abram saith unto the king of Sodom, 'I have lifted up my hand unto Jehovah, God Most High, possessing heaven and earth --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: from a thread even unto a shoe-latchet I take not of anything which thou hast, that thou say not, I -- I have made Abram rich;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: save only that which the young men have eaten, and the portion of the men who have gone with me -- Aner, Eshcol, and Mamre -- they take their portion.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: After these things hath the word of Jehovah been unto Abram in a vision, saying, 'Fear not, Abram, I [am] a shield to thee, thy reward [is] exceeding great.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Abram saith, 'Lord Jehovah, what dost Thou give to me, and I am going childless? and an acquired son in my house is Demmesek Eliezer.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Abram saith, 'Lo, to me Thou hast not given seed, and lo, a domestic doth heir me.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And lo, the word of Jehovah [is] unto him, saying, 'This [one] doth not heir thee; but he who cometh out from thy bowels, he doth heir thee;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and He bringeth him out without, and saith, 'Look attentively, I pray thee, towards the heavens, and count the stars, if thou art able to count them;' and He saith to him, 'Thus is thy seed.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he hath believed in Jehovah, and He reckoneth it to him -- righteousness.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And He saith unto him, 'I [am] Jehovah who brought thee out from Ur of the Chaldees, to give to thee this land to possess it;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he saith, 'Lord Jehovah, whereby do I know that I possess it?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And He saith unto him, 'Take for Me a heifer of three years, and a she-goat of three years, and a ram of three years, and a turtle-dove, and a young bird;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he taketh to him all these, and separateth them in the midst, and putteth each piece over against its fellow, but the bird he hath not divided;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the ravenous birds come down upon the carcases, and Abram causeth them to turn back.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the sun is about to go in, and deep sleep hath fallen upon Abram, and lo, a terror of great darkness is falling upon him;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and He saith to Abram, 'knowing -- know that thy seed is a sojourner in a land not theirs, and they have served them, and they have afflicted them four hundred years,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the nation also whom they serve I judge, and after this they go out with great substance;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and thou -- thou comest in unto thy fathers in peace; thou art buried in a good old age;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the fourth generation doth turn back hither, for the iniquity of the Amorite is not yet complete.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it cometh to pass -- the sun hath gone in, and thick darkness hath been -- and lo, a furnace of smoke, and a lamp of fire, which hath passed over between those pieces.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: In that day hath Jehovah made with Abram a covenant, saying, 'To thy seed I have given this land, from the river of Egypt unto the great river, the river Phrat,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: with the Kenite, and the Kenizzite, and the Kadmonite,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the Hittite, and the Perizzite, and the Rephaim,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the Amorite, and the Canaanite, and the Girgashite, and the Jebusite.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Sarai, Abram's wife, hath not borne to him, and she hath an handmaid, an Egyptian, and her name [is] Hagar;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Sarai saith unto Abram, 'Lo, I pray thee, Jehovah hath restrained me from bearing, go in, I pray thee, unto my handmaid; perhaps I am built up from her;' and Abram hearkeneth to the voice of Sarai.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Sarai, Abram's wife, taketh Hagar the Egyptian, her handmaid, at the end of the tenth year of Abram's dwelling in the land of Canaan, and giveth her to Abram her husband, to him for a wife,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he goeth in unto Hagar, and she conceiveth, and she seeth that she hath conceived, and her mistress is lightly esteemed in her eyes.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Sarai saith unto Abram, 'My violence [is] for thee; I -- I have given mine handmaid into thy bosom, and she seeth that she hath conceived, and I am lightly esteemed in her eyes; Jehovah doth judge between me and thee.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Abram saith unto Sarai, 'Lo, thine handmaid [is] in thine hand, do to her that which is good in thine eyes;' and Sarai afflicted her, and she fleeth from her presence.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And a messenger of Jehovah findeth her by the fountain of water in the wilderness, by the fountain in the way [to] Shur,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he saith, 'Hagar, Sarai's handmaid, whence hast thou come, and whither dost thou go?' and she saith, 'From the presence of Sarai, my mistress, I am fleeing.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the messenger of Jehovah saith to her, 'Turn back unto thy mistress, and humble thyself under her hands;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the messenger of Jehovah saith to her, 'Multiplying I multiply thy seed, and it is not numbered from multitude;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the messenger of Jehovah saith to her, 'Behold thou [art] conceiving, and bearing a son, and hast called his name Ishmael, for Jehovah hath hearkened unto thine affliction;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he is a wild-ass man, his hand against every one, and every one's hand against him -- and before the face of all his brethren he dwelleth.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And she calleth the name of Jehovah who is speaking unto her, 'Thou [art], O God, my beholder;' for she said, 'Even here have I looked behind my beholder?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: therefore hath one called the well, 'The well of the Living One, my beholder;' lo, between Kadesh and Bered.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Hagar beareth to Abram a son; and Abram calleth the name of his son, whom Hagar hath borne, Ishmael;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Abram [is] a son of eighty and six years in Hagar's bearing Ishmael to Abram.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Abram is a son of ninety and nine years, and Jehovah appeareth unto Abram, and saith unto him, 'I [am] God Almighty, walk habitually before Me, and be thou perfect;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and I give My covenant between Me and thee, and multiply thee very exceedingly.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Abram falleth upon his face, and God speaketh with him, saying,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'I -- lo, My covenant [is] with thee, and thou hast become father of a multitude of nations;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and thy name is no more called Abram, but thy name hath been Abraham, for father of a multitude of nations have I made thee;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and I have made thee exceeding fruitful, and made thee become nations, and kings go out from thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And I have established My covenant between Me and thee, and thy seed after thee, to their generations, for a covenant age-during, to become God to thee, and to thy seed after thee;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and I have given to thee, and to thy seed after thee, the land of thy sojournings, the whole land of Canaan, for a possession age-during, and I have become their God.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And God saith unto Abraham, 'And thou dost keep My covenant, thou and thy seed after thee, to their generations;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: this [is] My covenant which ye keep between Me and you, and thy seed after thee: Every male of you [is] to be circumcised;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and ye have circumcised the flesh of your foreskin, and it hath become a token of a covenant between Me and you.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And a son of eight days is circumcised by you; every male to your generations, born in the house, or bought with money from any son of a stranger, who is not of thy seed;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: he is certainly circumcised who [is] born in thine house, or bought with thy money; and My covenant hath become in your flesh a covenant age-during;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and an uncircumcised one, a male, the flesh of whose foreskin is not circumcised, even that person hath been cut off from his people; My covenant he hath broken.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And God saith unto Abraham, 'Sarai thy wife -- thou dost not call her name Sarai, for Sarah [is] her name;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and I have blessed her, and have also given to thee a son from her; and I have blessed her, and she hath become nations -- kings of peoples are from her.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Abraham falleth upon his face, and laugheth, and saith in his heart, 'To the son of an hundred years is one born? or doth Sarah -- daughter of ninety years -- bear?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Abraham saith unto God, 'O that Ishmael may live before Thee;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and God saith, 'Sarah thy wife is certainly bearing a son to thee, and thou hast called his name Isaac, and I have established My covenant with him, for a covenant age-during, to his seed after him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
