translations.text: As to Ishmael, I have heard thee; lo, I have blessed him, and made him fruitful, and multiplied him, very exceedingly; twelve princes doth he beget, and I have made him become a great nation;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and My covenant I establish with Isaac, whom Sarah doth bear to thee at this appointed time in the next year;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and He finisheth speaking with him, and God goeth up from Abraham.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Abraham taketh Ishmael his son, and all those born in his house, and all those bought with his money -- every male among the men of Abraham's house -- and circumciseth the flesh of their foreskin, in this self-same day, as God hath spoken with him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Abraham [is] a son of ninety and nine years in the flesh of his foreskin being circumcised;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Ishmael his son [is] a son of thirteen years in the flesh of his foreskin being circumcised;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: in this self-same day hath Abraham been circumcised, and Ishmael his son;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and all the men of his house -- born in the house, and bought with money from the son of a stranger -- have been circumcised with him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah appeareth unto him among the oaks of Mamre, and he is sitting at the opening of the tent, about the heat of the day;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he lifteth up his eyes and looketh, and lo, three men standing by him, and he seeth, and runneth to meet them from the opening of the tent, and boweth himself towards the earth,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he saith, 'My Lord, if, I pray thee, I have found grace in thine eyes, do not, I pray thee, pass on from thy servant;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: let, I pray thee, a little water be accepted, and wash your feet, and recline under the tree;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and I bring a piece of bread, and support ye your heart; afterwards pass on, for therefore have ye passed over unto your servant;' and they say, 'So mayest thou do as thou has spoken.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Abraham hasteth towards the tent, unto Sarah, and saith, 'Hasten three measures of flour-meal, knead, and make cakes;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Abraham ran unto the herd, and taketh a son of the herd, tender and good, and giveth unto the young man, and he hasteth to prepare it;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he taketh butter and milk, and the son of the herd which he hath prepared, and setteth before them; and he is standing by them under the tree, and they do eat.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they say unto him, 'Where [is] Sarah thy wife?' and he saith, 'Lo -- in the tent;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he saith, 'returning I return unto thee, about the time of life, and lo, to Sarah thy wife a son.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Sarah is hearkening at the opening of the tent, which is behind him;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Abraham and Sarah [are] aged, entering into days -- the way of women hath ceased to be to Sarah;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Sarah laugheth in her heart, saying, 'After I have waxed old I have had pleasure! -- my lord also [is] old!'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah saith unto Abraham, 'Why [is] this? Sarah hath laughed, saying, Is it true really -- I bear -- and I am aged? Is any thing too wonderful for Jehovah? at the appointed time I return unto thee, about the time of life, and Sarah hath a son.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Sarah denieth, saying, 'I did not laugh;' for she hath been afraid; and He saith, 'Nay, but thou didst laugh.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the men rise from thence, and look on the face of Sodom, and Abraham is going with them to send them away;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jehovah said, 'Am I concealing from Abraham that which I am doing,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Abraham certainly becometh a nation great and mighty, and blessed in him have been all nations of the earth?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for I have known him, that he commandeth his children, and his house after him (and they have kept the way of Jehovah), to do righteousness and judgment, that Jehovah may bring on Abraham that which He hath spoken concerning him.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah saith, 'The cry of Sodom and Gomorrah -- because great; and their sin -- because exceeding grievous:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I go down now, and see whether according to its cry which is coming unto Me they have done completely -- and if not -- I know;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the men turn from thence, and go towards Sodom; and Abraham is yet standing before Jehovah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Abraham draweth nigh and saith, 'Dost Thou also consume righteous with wicked?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: peradventure there are fifty righteous in the midst of the city; dost Thou also consume, and not bear with the place for the sake of the fifty -- the righteous who [are] in its midst?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Far be it from Thee to do according to this thing, to put to death the righteous with the wicked; that it hath been -- as the righteous so the wicked -- far be it from Thee; doth the Judge of all the earth not do justice?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah saith, 'If I find in Sodom fifty righteous in the midst of the city, then have I borne with all the place for their sake.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Abraham answereth and saith, 'Lo, I pray thee, I have willed to speak unto the Lord, and I -- dust and ashes;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: peradventure there are lacking five of the fifty righteous -- dost Thou destroy for five the whole of the city?' and He saith, 'I destroy [it] not, if I find there forty and five.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he addeth again to speak unto Him and saith, 'Peradventure there are found there forty?' and He saith, 'I do [it] not, because of the forty.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he saith, 'Let it not be, I Pray thee, displeasing to the Lord, and I speak: peradventure there are found there thirty?' and He saith, 'I do [it] not, if I find there thirty.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he saith, 'Lo, I pray thee, I have willed to speak unto the Lord: peradventure there are found there twenty?' and He saith, 'I do not destroy [it], because of the twenty.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he saith, 'Let it not be, I pray Thee, displeasing to the Lord, and I speak only this time: peradventure there are found there ten?' and He saith, 'I do not destroy [it], because of the ten.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah goeth on, when He hath finished speaking unto Abraham, and Abraham hath turned back to his place.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And two of the messengers come towards Sodom at even, and Lot is sitting at the gate of Sodom, and Lot seeth, and riseth to meet them, and boweth himself -- face to the earth,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he saith, 'Lo, I pray you, my lords, turn aside, I pray you, unto the house of your servant, and lodge, and wash your feet -- then ye have risen early and gone on your way;' and they say, 'Nay, but in the broad place we do lodge.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he presseth on them greatly, and they turn aside unto him, and come in unto his house; and he maketh for them a banquet, and hath baked unleavened things; and they do eat.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Before they lie down, the men of the city -- men of Sodom -- have come round about against the house, from young even unto aged, all the people from the extremity;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they call unto Lot and say to him, 'Where [are] the men who have come in unto thee to-night? bring them out unto us, and we know them.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Lot goeth out unto them, to the opening, and the door hath shut behind him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and saith, 'Do not, I pray you, my brethren, do evil;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: lo, I pray you, I have two daughters, who have not known any one; let me, I pray you, bring them out unto you, and do to them as [is] good in your eyes; only to these men do not anything, for therefore have they come in within the shadow of my roof.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they say, 'Come nigh hither;' they say also, 'This one hath come in to sojourn, and he certainly judgeth! now, we do evil to thee more than [to] them;' and they press against the man, against Lot greatly, and come nigh to break the door.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the men put forth their hand, and bring in Lot unto them, into the house, and have shut the door;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the men who [are] at the opening of the house they have smitten with blindness, from small even unto great, and they weary themselves to find the opening.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the men say unto Lot, 'Whom hast thou here still? son-in-law, thy sons also, and thy daughters, and all whom thou hast in the city, bring out from this place;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for we are destroying this place, for their cry hath been great [before] the face of Jehovah, and Jehovah doth send us to destroy it.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Lot goeth out, and speaketh unto his sons-in-law, those taking his daughters, and saith, 'Rise, go out from this place, for Jehovah is destroying the city;' and he is as [one] mocking in the eyes of his sons-in-law.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And when the dawn hath ascended, then the messengers press upon Lot, saying, 'Rise, take thy wife, and thy two daughters who are found present, lest thou be consumed in the iniquity of the city.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he lingereth, and the men lay hold on his hand, and on the hand of his wife, and on the hand of his two daughters, through the mercy of Jehovah unto him, and they bring him out, and cause him to rest without the city.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it cometh to pass when he hath brought them out without, that he saith, 'Escape for thy life; look not expectingly behind thee, nor stand thou in all the circuit; to the mountain escape, lest thou be consumed.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Lot saith unto them, 'Not [so], I pray thee, my lord;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: lo, I pray thee, thy servant hath found grace in thine eyes, and thou dost make great thy kindness which thou hast done with me by saving my life, and I am unable to escape to the mountain, lest the evil cleave [to] me, and I have died;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: lo, I pray thee, this city [is] near to flee thither, and it [is] little; let me escape, I pray thee, thither, (is it not little?) and my soul doth live.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he saith unto him, 'Lo, I have accepted thy face also for this thing, without overthrowing the city [for] which thou hast spoken;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: haste, escape thither, for I am not able to do anything till thine entering thither;' therefore hath he calleth the name of the city Zoar.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The sun hath gone out on the earth, and Lot hath entered into Zoar,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jehovah hath rained upon Sodom and upon Gomorrah brimstone and fire from Jehovah, from the heavens;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and He overthroweth these cities, and all the circuit, and all the inhabitants of the cities, and that which is shooting up from the ground.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And his wife looketh expectingly from behind him, and she is -- a pillar of salt!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Abraham riseth early in the morning, unto the place where he hath stood [before] the face of Jehovah;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he looketh on the face of Sodom and Gomorrah, and on all the face of the land of the circuit, and seeth, and lo, the smoke of the land went up as smoke of the furnace.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it cometh to pass, in God's destroying the cities of the circuit, that God remembereth Abraham, and sendeth Lot out of the midst of the overthrow in the overthrowing of the cities in which Lot dwelt.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Lot goeth up out of Zoar, and dwelleth in the mountain, and his two daughters with him, for he hath been afraid of dwelling in Zoar, and he dwelleth in a cave, he and his two daughters.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the first-born saith unto the younger, 'Our father [is] old, and a man there is not in the earth to come in unto us, as [is] the way of all the earth;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: come, we cause our father to drink wine, and lie with him, and preserve from our father -- a seed.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they cause their father to drink wine on that night; and the first-born goeth in, and lieth with her father, and he hath not known in her lying down, or in her rising up.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it cometh to pass, on the morrow, that the first-born saith unto the younger, 'Lo, I have lain yesterday-night with my father: we cause him to drink wine also to-night, and go thou in, lie with him, and we preserve from our father -- a seed.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they cause their father to drink wine on that night also, and the younger riseth and lieth with him, and he hath not known in her lying down, or in her rising up.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the two daughters of Lot conceive from their father,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the first-born beareth a son, and calleth his name Moab; he [is] father of Moab unto this day;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: as to the younger, she also hath born a son, and calleth his name Ben-Ammi: he [is] father of the Beni-Ammon unto this day.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Abraham journeyeth from thence toward the land of the south, and dwelleth between Kadesh and Shur, and sojourneth in Gerar;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Abraham saith concerning Sarah his wife, 'She is my sister;' and Abimelech king of Gerar sendeth and taketh Sarah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And God cometh in unto Abimelech in a dream of the night, and saith to him, 'Lo, thou [art] a dead man, because of the woman whom thou hast taken -- and she married to a husband.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Abimelech hath not drawn near unto her, and he saith, 'Lord, also a righteous nation dost thou slay?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: hath not he himself said to me, She [is] my sister! and she, even she herself, said, He [is] my brother; in the integrity of my heart, and in the innocency of my hands, I have done this.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And God saith unto him in the dream, 'Yea, I -- I have known that in the integrity of thy heart thou hast done this, and I withhold thee, even I, from sinning against Me, therefore I have not suffered thee to come against her;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and now send back the man's wife, for he [is] inspired, and he doth pray for thee, and live thou; and if thou do not send back, know that dying thou dost die, thou, and all that thou hast.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Abimelech riseth early in the morning, and calleth for all his servants, and speaketh all these words in their ears; and the men fear exceedingly;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Abimelech calleth for Abraham, and saith to him, 'What hast thou done to us? and what have I sinned against thee, that thou hast brought upon me, and upon my kingdom, a great sin? works which are not done thou hast done with me.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Abimelech also saith unto Abraham, 'What hast thou seen that thou hast done this thing?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Abraham saith, 'Because I said, 'Surely the fear of God is not in this place, and they have slain me for the sake of my wife;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and also, truly she is my sister, daughter of my father, only not daughter of my mother, and she becometh my wife;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and it cometh to pass, when God hath caused me to wander from my father's house, that I say to her, This [is] thy kindness which thou dost with me: at every place whither we come, say of me, He [is] my brother.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Abimelech taketh sheep and oxen, and servants and handmaids, and giveth to Abraham, and sendeth back to him Sarah his wife;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Abimelech saith, 'Lo, my land [is] before thee, where it is good in thine eyes, dwell;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and to Sarah he hath said, 'Lo, I have given a thousand silverlings to thy brother; lo, it is to thee a covering of eyes, to all who are with thee;' and by all this she is reasoned with.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Abraham prayeth unto God, and God healeth Abimelech and his wife, and his handmaids, and they bear:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for Jehovah restraining had restrained every womb of the house of Abimelech, because of Sarah, Abraham's wife.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah hath looked after Sarah as He hath said, and Jehovah doth to Sarah as He hath spoken;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Sarah conceiveth, and beareth a son to Abraham, to his old age, at the appointed time that God hath spoken of with him;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Abraham calleth the name of his son who is born to him, whom Sarah hath born to him -- Isaac;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Abraham circumciseth Isaac his son, [being] a son of eight days, as God hath commanded him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Abraham [is] a son of a hundred years in Isaac his son being born to him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Sarah saith, 'God hath made laughter for me; every one who is hearing laugheth for me.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: She saith also, 'Who hath said to Abraham, Sarah hath suckled sons, that I have born a son for his old age?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the lad groweth, and is weaned, and Abraham maketh a great banquet in the day of Isaac's being weaned;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Sarah seeth the son of Hagar the Egyptian, whom she hath borne to Abraham, mocking,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and she saith to Abraham, 'Cast out this handmaid and her son; for the son of this handmaid hath no possession with my son -- with Isaac.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the thing is very wrong in the eyes of Abraham, for his son's sake;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and God saith unto Abraham, 'Let it not be wrong in thine eyes because of the youth, and because of thy handmaid: all that Sarah saith unto thee -- hearken to her voice, for in Isaac is a seed called to thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: As to the son of the handmaid also, for a nation I set him, because he [is] thy seed.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Abraham riseth early in the morning, and taketh bread, and a bottle of water, and giveth unto Hagar (placing [it] on her shoulder), also the lad, and sendeth her out; and she goeth on, and goeth astray in the wilderness of Beer-Sheba;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the water is consumed from the bottle, and she placeth the lad under one of the shrubs.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And she goeth and sitteth by herself over-against, afar off, about a bow-shot, for she said, 'Let me not look on the death of the lad;' and she sitteth over-against, and lifteth up her voice, and weepeth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And God heareth the voice of the youth; and the messenger of God calleth unto Hagar from the heavens, and saith to her, 'What to thee, Hagar? fear not; for God hath hearkened unto the voice of the youth where he [is];

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: rise, lift up the youth, and lay hold on him with thy hand, for for a great nation I set him.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And God openeth her eyes, and she seeth a well of water, and she goeth and filleth the bottle [with] water, and causeth the youth to drink;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and God is with the youth, and he groweth, and dwelleth in the wilderness, and is an archer;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he dwelleth in the wilderness of Paran, and his mother taketh for him a wife from the land of Egypt.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it cometh to pass at that time that Abimelech speaketh -- Phichol also, head of his host -- unto Abraham, saying, 'God [is] with thee in all that thou art doing;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and now, swear to me by God here: thou dost not lie to me, or to my continuator, or to my successor; according to the kindness which I have done with thee thou dost with me, and with the land in which thou hast sojourned.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Abraham saith, 'I -- I do swear.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Abraham reasoned with Abimelech concerning the matter of a well of water which Abimelech's servants have taken violently away,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Abimelech saith, 'I have not known who hath done this thing, and even thou didst not declare to me, and I also, I have not heard save to-day.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Abraham taketh sheep and oxen, and giveth to Abimelech, and they make, both of them, a covenant;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Abraham setteth seven Lambs of the flock by themselves.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Abimelech saith unto Abraham, 'What [are] they -- these seven lambs which thou hast set by themselves?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he saith, 'For -- the seven lambs thou dost accept from my hand, so that it becometh a witness for me that I have digged this well;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: therefore hath he called that place 'Beer-Sheba,' for there have both of them sworn.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they make a covenant in Beer-Sheba, and Abimelech riseth -- Phichol also, head of his host -- and they turn back unto the land of the Philistines;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and [Abraham] planteth a tamarisk in Beer-Sheba, and preacheth there in the name of Jehovah, God age-during;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Abraham sojourneth in the land of the Philistines many days.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it cometh to pass after these things that God hath tried Abraham, and saith unto him, 'Abraham;' and he saith, 'Here [am] I.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And He saith, 'Take, I pray thee, thy son, thine only one, whom thou hast loved, even Isaac, and go for thyself unto the land of Moriah, and cause him to ascend there for a burnt-offering on one of the mountains of which I speak unto thee.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Abraham riseth early in the morning, and saddleth his ass, and taketh two of his young men with him, and Isaac his son, and he cleaveth the wood of the burnt-offering, and riseth and goeth unto the place of which God hath spoken to him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: On the third day -- Abraham lifteth up his eyes, and seeth the place from afar;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Abraham saith unto his young men, 'Remain by yourselves here with the ass, and I and the youth go yonder and worship, and turn back unto you.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Abraham taketh the wood of the burnt-offering, and placeth on Isaac his son, and he taketh in his hand the fire, and the knife; and they go on both of them together.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Isaac speaketh unto Abraham his father, and saith, 'My father,' and he saith, 'Here [am] I, my son.' And he saith, 'Lo, the fire and the wood, and where the lamb for a burnt-offering?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Abraham saith, 'God doth provide for Himself the lamb for a burnt-offering, my son;' and they go on both of them together.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they come in unto the place of which God hath spoken to him, and there Abraham buildeth the altar, and arrangeth the wood, and bindeth Isaac his son, and placeth him upon the altar above the wood;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Abraham putteth forth his hand, and taketh the knife -- to slaughter his son.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the messenger of Jehovah calleth unto him from the heavens, and saith, 'Abraham, Abraham;' and he saith, 'Here [am] I;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and He saith, 'Put not forth thine hand unto the youth, nor do anything to him, for now I have known that thou art fearing God, and hast not withheld thy son, thine only one, from Me.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Abraham lifteth up his eyes, and looketh, and lo, a ram behind, seized in a thicket by its horns; and Abraham goeth, and taketh the ram, and causeth it to ascend for a burnt-offering instead of his son;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Abraham calleth the name of that place 'Jehovah-Jireh,' because it is said this day in the mount, 'Jehovah doth provide.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the messenger of Jehovah calleth unto Abraham a second time from the heavens,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and saith, 'By Myself I have sworn -- the affirmation of Jehovah -- that because thou hast done this thing, and hast not withheld thy son, thine only one --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: that blessing I bless thee, and multiplying I multiply thy seed as stars of the heavens, and as sand which [is] on the sea-shore; and thy seed doth possess the gate of his enemies;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and blessed themselves in thy seed have all nations of the earth, because that thou hast hearkened to My voice.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Abraham turneth back unto his young men, and they rise and go together unto Beer-Sheba; and Abraham dwelleth in Beer-Sheba.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it cometh to pass after these things that it is declared to Abraham, saying, 'Lo, Milcah hath borne, even she, sons to Nahor thy brother:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Huz his first-born, and Buz his brother; and Kemuel father of Aram,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Chesed, and Hazo, and Pildash, and Jidlaph, and Bethuel;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Bethuel hath begotten Rebekah;' these eight hath Milcah borne to Nahor, Abraham's brother;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and his concubine, whose name [is] Reumah, she also hath borne Tebah, and Gaham, and Tahash, and Maachah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the life of Sarah is a hundred and twenty and seven years -- years of the life of Sarah;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Sarah dieth in Kirjath-Arba, which [is] Hebron, in the land of Caanan, and Abraham goeth in to mourn for Sarah, and to bewail her.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Abraham riseth up from the presence of his dead, and speaketh unto the sons of Heth, saying,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'A sojourner and a settler I [am] with you; give to me a possession of a burying-place with you, and I bury my dead from before me.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the sons of Heth answer Abraham, saying to him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Hear us, my lord; a prince of God [art] thou in our midst; in the choice of our burying-places bury thy dead: none of us his burying-place doth withhold from thee, from burying thy dead.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Abraham riseth and boweth himself to the people of the land, to the sons of Heth,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he speaketh with them, saying, 'If it is your desire to bury my dead from before me, hear me, and meet for me with Ephron, son of Zoar;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he giveth to me the cave of Machpelah, which he hath, which [is] in the extremity of his field; for full money doth he give it to me, in your midst, for a possession of a burying-place.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Ephron is sitting in the midst of the sons of Heth, and Ephron the Hittite answereth Abraham in the ears of the sons of Heth, of all those entering the gate of his city, saying,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Nay, my lord, hear me: the field I have given to thee, and the cave that [is] in it, to thee I have given it; before the eyes of the sons of my people I have given it to thee -- bury thy dead.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Abraham boweth himself before the people of the land,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and speaketh unto Ephron in the ears of the people of the land, saying, 'Only -- if thou wouldest hear me -- I have given the money of the field -- accept from me, and I bury my dead there.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Ephron answereth Abraham, saying to him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'My lord, hear me: the land -- four hundred shekels of silver; between me and thee, what [is] it? -- thy dead bury.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Abraham hearkeneth unto Ephron, and Abraham weigheth to Ephron the silver which he hath spoken of in the ears of the sons of Heth, four hundred silver shekels, passing with the merchant.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And established are the field of Ephron, which [is] in Machpelah, which [is] before Mamre, the field and the cave which [is] in it, and all the trees which [are] in the field, which [are] in all its border round about,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: to Abraham by purchase, before the eyes of the sons of Heth, among all entering the gate of his city.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And after this hath Abraham buried Sarah his wife at the cave of the field of Machpelah before Mamre (which [is] Hebron), in the land of Canaan;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and established are the field, and the cave which [is] in it, to Abraham for a possession of a burying-place, from the sons of Heth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Abraham [is] old, he hath entered into days, and Jehovah hath blessed Abraham in all [things];

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Abraham saith unto his servant, the eldest of his house, who is ruling over all that he hath, 'Put, I pray thee, thy hand under my thigh,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and I cause thee to swear by Jehovah, God of the heavens, and God of the earth, that thou dost not take a wife for my son from the daughters of the Canaanite, in the midst of whom I am dwelling;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but unto my land and unto my kindred dost thou go, and hast taken a wife for my son, for Isaac.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the servant saith unto him, 'It may be the woman is not willing to come after me unto this land; do I at all cause thy son to turn back unto the land from whence thou camest out?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Abraham saith unto him, 'Take heed to thyself, lest thou cause my son to turn back thither;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Jehovah, God of the heavens, who hath taken me from the house of my father, and from the land of my birth, and who hath spoken to me, and who hath sworn to me, saying, To thy seed I give this land, He doth send His messenger before thee, and thou hast taken a wife for my son from thence;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and if the woman be not willing to come after thee, then thou hast been acquitted from this mine oath: only my son thou dost not cause to turn back thither.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the servant putteth his hand under the thigh of Abraham his lord, and sweareth to him concerning this matter.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the servant taketh ten camels of the camels of his lord and goeth, also of all the goods of his lord in his hand, and he riseth, and goeth unto Aram-Naharaim, unto the city of Nahor;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he causeth the camels to kneel at the outside of the city, at the well of water, at even-time, at the time of the coming out of the women who draw water.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he saith, 'Jehovah, God of my lord Abraham, cause to meet, I pray Thee, before me this day -- (and do kindness with my lord Abraham;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: lo, I am standing by the fountain of water, and daughters of the men of the city are coming out to draw water;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and it hath been, the young person unto whom I say, Incline, I pray thee, thy pitcher, and I drink, and she hath said, Drink, and I water also thy camels) -- her Thou hast decided for Thy servant, for Isaac; and by it I know that Thou hast done kindness with my lord.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it cometh to pass, before he hath finished speaking, that lo, Rebekah (who was born to Bethuel, son of Milcah, wife of Nahor, brother of Abraham) is coming out, and her pitcher on her shoulder,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the young person [is] of very good appearance, a virgin, and a man hath not known her; and she goeth down to the fountain, and filleth her pitcher, and cometh up.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the servant runneth to meet her, and saith, 'Let me swallow, I pray thee, a little water from thy pitcher;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and she saith, 'Drink, my lord;' and she hasteth, and letteth down her pitcher upon her hand, and giveth him drink.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And she finisheth giving him drink, and saith, 'Also for thy camels I draw till they have finished drinking;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and she hasteth, and emptieth her pitcher into the drinking-trough, and runneth again unto the well to draw, and draweth for all his camels.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the man, wondering at her, remaineth silent, to know whether Jehovah hath made his way prosperous or not.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it cometh to pass when the camels have finished drinking, that the man taketh a golden ring (whose weight [is] a bekah), and two bracelets for her hands (whose weight [is] ten [bekahs] of gold),

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and saith, 'Whose daughter [art] thou? declare to me, I pray thee, is the house of thy father a place for us to lodge in?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And she saith unto him, 'I [am] daughter of Bethuel, son of Milcah, whom she hath borne to Nahor.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: She saith also unto him, 'Both straw and provender [are] abundant with us, also a place to lodge in.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the man boweth, and doth obeisance to Jehovah,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and saith, 'Blessed [is] Jehovah, God of my lord Abraham, who hath not left off His kindness and His truth with my lord; -- I [being] in the way, Jehovah hath led me to the house of my lord's brethren.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the young person runneth, and declareth to the house of her mother according to these words.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Rebekah hath a brother, and his name [is] Laban, and Laban runneth unto the man who [is] without, unto the fountain;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: yea, it cometh to pass, when he seeth the ring, and the bracelets on the hands of his sister, and when he heareth the words of Rebekah his sister, saying, 'Thus hath the man spoken unto me,' that he cometh in unto the man, and lo, he is standing by the camels by the fountain.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he saith, 'Come in, O blessed one of Jehovah, why standest thou without, and I -- I have prepared the house and place for the camels!'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he bringeth in the man into the house, and looseth the camels, and giveth straw and provender for the camels, and water to wash his feet, and the feet of the men who [are] with him:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and setteth before him to eat; but he saith, 'I do not eat till I have spoken my word;' and he saith, 'Speak.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he saith, 'I [am] Abraham's servant;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jehovah hath blessed my lord exceedingly, and he is great; and He giveth to him flock, and herd, and silver, and gold, and men-servants, and maid-servants, and camels, and asses;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Sarah, my lord's wife, beareth a son to my lord, after she hath been aged, and he giveth to him all that he hath.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And my lord causeth me to swear, saying, Thou dost not take a wife to my son from the daughters of the Canaanite, in whose land I am dwelling.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: If not -- unto the house of my father thou dost go, and unto my family, and thou hast taken a wife for my son.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And I say unto my lord, It may be the woman doth not come after me;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he saith unto me, Jehovah, before whom I have walked habitually, doth send His messenger with thee, and hath prospered thy way, and thou hast taken a wife for my son from my family, and from the house of my father;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: then art thou acquitted from my oath, when thou comest unto my family, and if they give not [one] to thee; then thou hast been acquitted from my oath.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And I come to-day unto the fountain, and I say, Jehovah, God of my lord Abraham, if Thou art, I pray Thee, making prosperous my way in which I am going --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: (lo, I am standing by the fountain of water), then the virgin who is coming out to draw, and I have said unto her, Let me drink, I pray thee, a little water from thy pitcher,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and she hath said unto me, Both drink thou, and also for thy camels I draw -- she is the woman whom Jehovah hath decided for my lord's son.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Before I finish speaking unto my heart, then lo, Rebekah is coming out, and her pitcher on her shoulder, and she goeth down to the fountain, and draweth; and I say unto her, Let me drink, I pray thee,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and she hasteth and letteth down her pitcher from off her and saith, Drink, and thy camels also I water; and I drink, and the camels also she hath watered.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And I ask her, and say, Whose daughter [art] thou? and she saith, Daughter of Bethuel, son of Nahor, whom Milcah hath borne to him, and I put the ring on her nose, and the bracelets on her hands,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and I bow, and do obeisance before Jehovah, and I bless Jehovah, God of my lord Abraham, who hath led me in the true way to receive the daughter of my lord's brother for his son.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And now, if ye are dealing kindly and truly with my lord, declare to me; and if not, declare to me; and I turn unto the right or unto the left.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Laban answereth -- Bethuel also -- and they say, 'The thing hath gone out from Jehovah; we are not able to speak unto thee bad or good;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: lo, Rebekah [is] before thee, take and go, and she is a wife to thy lord's son, as Jehovah hath spoken.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it cometh to pass, when the servant of Abraham hath heard their words, that he boweth himself towards the earth before Jehovah;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the servant taketh out vessels of silver, and vessels of gold, and garments, and giveth to Rebekah; precious things also he hath given to her brother and to her mother.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they eat and drink, he and the men who [are] with him, and lodge all night; and they rise in the morning, and he saith, 'Send me to my lord;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and her brother saith -- her mother also -- 'Let the young person abide with us a week or ten days, afterwards doth she go.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he saith unto them, 'Do not delay me, seeing Jehovah hath prospered my way; send me away, and I go to my lord;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they say, 'Let us call for the young person, and ask at her mouth;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they call for Rebekah, and say unto her, 'Dost thou go with this man?' and she saith, 'I go.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they send away Rebekah their sister, and her nurse, and Abraham's servant, and his men;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they bless Rebekah, and say to her, 'Thou [art] our sister; become thou thousands of myriads, and thy seed doth possess the gate of those hating it.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Rebekah and her young women arise, and ride on the camels, and go after the man; and the servant taketh Rebekah and goeth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Isaac hath come in from the entrance of the Well of the Living One, my Beholder; and he is dwelling in the land of the south,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Isaac goeth out to meditate in the field, at the turning of the evening, and he lifteth up his eyes, and looketh, and lo, camels are coming.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Rebekah lifteth up her eyes, and seeth Isaac, and alighteth from off the camel;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and she saith unto the servant, 'Who [is] this man who is walking in the field to meet us?' and the servant saith, 'It [is] my lord;' and she taketh the veil, and covereth herself.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the servant recounteth to Isaac all the things that he hath done,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Isaac bringeth her in unto the tent of Sarah his mother, and he taketh Rebekah, and she becometh his wife, and he loveth her, and Isaac is comforted after [the death of] his mother.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Abraham addeth and taketh a wife, and her name [is] Keturah;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and she beareth to him Zimran, and Jokshan, and Medan, and Midian, and Ishbak, and Shuah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jokshan hath begotten Sheba and Dedan; and the sons of Dedan were Asshurim, and Letushim, and Leummim;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the sons of Midian [are] Ephah, and Epher, and Hanoch, and Abidah, and Eldaah: all these [are] sons of Keturah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Abraham giveth all that he hath to Isaac;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and to the sons of the concubines whom Abraham hath, Abraham hath given gifts, and sendeth them away from Isaac his son (in his being yet alive) eastward, unto the east country.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And these [are] the days of the years of the life of Abraham, which he lived, a hundred and seventy and five years;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Abraham expireth, and dieth in a good old age, aged and satisfied, and is gathered unto his people.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Isaac and Ishmael his sons bury him at the cave of Machpelah, at the field of Ephron, son of Zoar the Hittite, which [is] before Mamre --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: the field which Abraham bought from the sons of Heth -- there hath Abraham been buried, and Sarah his wife.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it cometh to pass after the death of Abraham, that God blesseth Isaac his son; and Isaac dwelleth by the Well of the Living One, my Beholder.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And these [are] births of Ishmael, Abraham's son, whom Hagar the Egyptian, Sarah's handmaid, hath borne to Abraham;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and these [are] the names of the sons of Ishmael, by their names, according to their births: first-born of Ishmael, Nebajoth; and Kedar, and Adbeel, and Mibsam,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Mishma, and Dumah, and Massa,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Hadar, and Tema, Jetur, Naphish, and Kedemah:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: these are sons of Ishmael, and these their names, by their villages, and by their towers; twelve princes according to their peoples.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And these [are] the years of the life of Ishmael, a hundred and thirty and seven years; and he expireth, and dieth, and is gathered unto his people;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they tabernacle from Havilah unto Shur, which [is] before Egypt, in [thy] going towards Asshur; in the presence of all his brethren hath he fallen.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And these [are] births of Isaac, Abraham's son: Abraham hath begotten Isaac;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Isaac is a son of forty years in his taking Rebekah, daughter of Bethuel the Aramaean, from Padan-Aram, sister of Laban the Aramaean, to him for a wife.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Isaac maketh entreaty to Jehovah before his wife, for she [is] barren: and Jehovah is entreated of him, and Rebekah his wife conceiveth,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the children struggle together within her, and she saith, 'If [it is] right -- why [am] I thus?' and she goeth to seek Jehovah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah saith to her, 'Two nations [are] in thy womb, and two peoples from thy bowels are parted; and the [one] people than the [other] people is stronger; and the elder doth serve the younger.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And her days to bear are fulfilled, and lo, twins [are] in her womb;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the first cometh out all red as a hairy robe, and they call his name Esau;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and afterwards hath his brother come out, and his hand is taking hold on Esau's heel, and one calleth his name Jacob; and Isaac [is] a son of sixty years in her bearing them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the youths grew, and Esau is a man acquainted [with] hunting, a man of the field; and Jacob [is] a plain man, inhabiting tents;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Isaac loveth Esau, for [his] hunting [is] in his mouth; and Rebekah is loving Jacob.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jacob boileth pottage, and Esau cometh in from the field, and he [is] weary;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Esau saith unto Jacob, 'Let me eat, I pray thee, some of this red red thing, for I [am] weary;' therefore hath [one] called his name Edom [Red];

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jacob saith, 'Sell to-day thy birthright to me.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Esau saith, 'Lo, I am going to die, and what is this to me -- birthright?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jacob saith, 'Swear to me to-day:' and he sweareth to him, and selleth his birthright to Jacob;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jacob hath given to Esau bread and pottage of lentiles, and he eateth, and drinketh, and riseth, and goeth; and Esau despiseth the birthright.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And there is a famine in the land, besides the first famine which was in the days of Abraham, and Isaac goeth unto Abimelech king of the Philistines, to Gerar.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah appeareth unto him, and saith, 'Go not down towards Egypt, tabernacle in the land concerning which I speak unto thee,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: sojourn in this land, and I am with thee, and bless thee, for to thee and to thy seed I give all these lands, and I have established the oath which I have sworn to Abraham thy father;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and I have multiplied thy seed as stars of the heavens, and I have given to thy seed all these lands; and blessed themselves in thy seed have all nations of the earth;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: because that Abraham hath hearkened to My voice, and keepeth My charge, My commands, My statutes, and My laws.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Isaac dwelleth in Gerar;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and men of the place ask him of his wife, and he saith, 'She [is] my sister:' for he hath been afraid to say, 'My wife -- lest the men of the place kill me for Rebekah, for she [is] of good appearance.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it cometh to pass, when the days have been prolonged to him there, that Abimelech king of the Philistines looketh through the window, and seeth, and lo, Isaac is playing with Rebekah his wife.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Abimelech calleth for Isaac, and saith, 'Lo, she [is] surely thy wife; and how hast thou said, She [is] my sister?' and Isaac saith unto him, 'Because I said, Lest I die for her.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Abimelech saith, 'What [is] this thou hast done to us? as a little thing one of the people had lain with thy wife, and thou hadst brought upon us guilt;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Abimelech commandeth all the people, saying, 'He who cometh against this man or against his wife, dying doth die.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Isaac soweth in that land, and findeth in that year a hundredfold, and Jehovah blesseth him;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the man is great, and goeth on, going on and becoming great, till that he hath been very great,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he hath possession of a flock, and possession of a herd, and an abundant service; and the Philistines envy him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and all the wells which his father's servants digged in the days of Abraham his father, the Philistines have stopped them, and fill them with dust.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Abimelech saith unto Isaac, 'Go from us; for thou hast become much mightier than we;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Isaac goeth from thence, and encampeth in the valley of Gerar, and dwelleth there;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Isaac turneth back, and diggeth the wells of water which they digged in the days of Abraham his father, which the Philistines do stop after the death of Abraham, and he calleth to them names according to the names which his father called them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Isaac's servants dig in the valley, and find there a well of living water,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and shepherds of Gerar strive with shepherds of Isaac, saying, 'The water [is] ours;' and he calleth the name of the well 'Strife,' because they have striven habitually with him;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they dig another well, and they strive also for it, and he calleth its name 'Hatred.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he removeth from thence, and diggeth another well, and they have not striven for it, and he calleth its name Enlargements, and saith, 'For -- now hath Jehovah given enlargement to us, and we have been fruitful in the land.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he goeth up from thence [to] Beer-Sheba,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jehovah appeareth unto him during that night, and saith, 'I [am] the God of Abraham thy father, fear not, for I [am] with thee, and have blessed thee, and have multiplied thy seed, because of Abraham My servant;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he buildeth there an altar, and preacheth in the name of Jehovah, and stretcheth out there his tent, and there Isaac's servants dig a well.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Abimelech hath gone unto him from Gerar, and Ahuzzath his friend, and Phichol head of his host;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Isaac saith unto them, 'Wherefore have ye come unto me, and ye have hated me, and ye send me away from you?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they say, 'We have certainly seen that Jehovah hath been with thee, and we say, 'Let there be, we pray thee, an oath between us, between us and thee, and let us make a covenant with thee;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: do not evil with us, as we have not touched thee, and as we have only done good with thee, and send thee away in peace; thou [art] now blessed of Jehovah.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he maketh for them a banquet, and they eat and drink,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and rise early in the morning, and swear one to another, and Isaac sendeth them away, and they go from him in peace.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it cometh to pass during that day that Isaac's servants come and declare to him concerning the circumstances of the well which they have digged, and say to him, 'We have found water;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he calleth it Shebah, [oath,] therefore the name of the city [is] Beer-Sheba, [well of the oath,] unto this day.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Esau is a son of forty years, and he taketh a wife, Judith, daughter of Beeri the Hittite, and Bashemath, daughter of Elon the Hittite,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they are a bitterness of spirit to Isaac and to Rebekah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it cometh to pass that Isaac [is] aged, and his eyes are too dim for seeing, and he calleth Esau his elder son, and saith unto him, 'My son;' and he saith unto him, 'Here [am] I.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he saith, 'Lo, I pray thee, I have become aged, I have not known the day of my death;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and now, take up, I pray thee, thy instruments, thy quiver, and thy bow, and go out to the field, and hunt for me provision,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and make for me tasteful things, [such] as I have loved, and bring in to me, and I do eat, so that my soul doth bless thee before I die.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Rebekah is hearkening while Isaac is speaking unto Esau his son; and Esau goeth to the field to hunt provision -- to bring in;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Rebekah hath spoken unto Jacob her son, saying, 'Lo, I have heard thy father speaking unto Esau thy brother, saying,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Bring for me provision, and make for me tasteful things, and I do eat, and bless thee before Jehovah before my death.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And now, my son, hearken to my voice, to that which I am commanding thee:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Go, I pray thee, unto the flock, and take for me from thence two good kids of the goats, and I make them tasteful things for thy father, [such] as he hath loved;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and thou hast taken in to thy father, and he hath eaten, so that his soul doth bless thee before his death.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jacob saith unto Rebekah his mother, 'Lo, Esau my brother [is] a hairy man, and I a smooth man,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: it may be my father doth feel me, and I have been in his eyes as a deceiver, and have brought upon me disesteem, and not a blessing;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and his mother saith to him, 'On me thy disesteem, my son; only hearken to my voice, and go, take for me.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he goeth, and taketh, and bringeth to his mother, and his mother maketh tasteful things, [such] as his father hath loved;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Rebekah taketh the desirable garments of Esau her elder son, which [are] with her in the house, and doth put on Jacob her younger son;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the skins of the kids of the goats she hath put on his hands, and on the smooth of his neck,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and she giveth the tasteful things, and the bread which she hath made, into the hand of Jacob her son.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he cometh in unto his father, and saith, 'My father;' and he saith, 'Here [am] I; who [art] thou, my son?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jacob saith unto his father, 'I [am] Esau thy first-born; I have done as thou hast spoken unto me; rise, I pray thee, sit, and eat of my provision, so that thy soul doth bless me.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Isaac saith unto his son, 'What [is] this thou hast hasted to find, my son?' and he saith, 'That which Jehovah thy God hath caused to come before me.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Isaac saith unto Jacob, 'Come nigh, I pray thee, and I feel thee, my son, whether thou [art] he, my son Esau, or not.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jacob cometh nigh unto Isaac his father, and he feeleth him, and saith, 'The voice [is] the voice of Jacob, and the hands hands of Esau.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he hath not discerned him, for his hands have been hairy, as the hands of Esau his brother, and he blesseth him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and saith, 'Thou art he -- my son Esau?' and he saith, 'I [am].'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he saith, 'Bring nigh to me, and I do eat of my son's provision, so that my soul doth bless thee;' and he bringeth nigh to him, and he eateth; and he bringeth to him wine, and he drinketh.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Isaac his father saith to him, 'Come nigh, I pray thee, and kiss me, my son;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he cometh nigh, and kisseth him, and he smelleth the fragrance of his garments, and blesseth him, and saith, 'See, the fragrance of my son [is] as the fragrance of a field which Jehovah hath blessed;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and God doth give to thee of the dew of heaven, and of the fatness of the earth, and abundance of corn and wine;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: peoples serve thee, and nations bow themselves to thee, be thou mighty over thy brethren, and the sons of thy mother bow themselves to thee; those who curse thee [are] cursed, and those who bless thee [are] blessed.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it cometh to pass, as Isaac hath finished blessing Jacob, and Jacob is only just going out from the presence of Isaac his father, that Esau his brother hath come in from his hunting;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he also maketh tasteful things, and bringeth to his father, and saith to his father, 'Let my father arise, and eat of his son's provision, so that thy soul doth bless me.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Isaac his father saith to him, 'Who [art] thou?' and he saith, 'I [am] thy son, thy first-born, Esau;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Isaac trembleth a very great trembling, and saith, 'Who, now, [is] he who hath provided provision, and bringeth in to me, and I eat of all before thou comest in, and I bless him? -- yea, blessed is he.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: When Esau heareth the words of his father, then he crieth a very great and bitter cry, and saith to his father, 'Bless me, me also, O my father;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he saith, 'Thy brother hath come with subtilty, and taketh thy blessing.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he saith, 'Is it because [one] called his name Jacob that he doth take me by the heel these two times? my birthright he hath taken; and lo, now, he hath taken my blessing;' he saith also, 'Hast thou not kept back a blessing for me?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Isaac answereth and saith to Esau, 'Lo, a mighty one have I set him over thee, and all his brethren have I given to him for servants, and [with] corn and wine have I sustained him; and for thee now, what shall I do, my son?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Esau saith unto his father, 'One blessing hast thou my father? bless me, me also, O my father;' and Esau lifteth up his voice, and weepeth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Isaac his father answereth and saith unto him, 'Lo, of the fatness of the earth is thy dwelling, and of the dew of the heavens from above;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and by thy sword dost thou live, and thy brother dost thou serve; and it hath come to pass when thou rulest, that thou hast broken his yoke from off thy neck.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Esau hateth Jacob, because of the blessing with which his father blessed him, and Esau saith in his heart, 'The days of mourning [for] my father draw near, and I slay Jacob my brother.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the words of Esau her elder son are declared to Rebekah, and she sendeth and calleth for Jacob her younger son, and saith unto him, 'Lo, Esau thy brother is comforting himself in regard to thee -- to slay thee;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and now, my son, hearken to my voice, and rise, flee for thyself unto Laban my brother, to Haran,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and thou hast dwelt with him some days, till thy brother's fury turn back,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: till thy brother's anger turn back from thee, and he hath forgotten that which thou hast done to him, and I have sent and taken thee from thence; why am I bereaved even of you both the same day?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Rebekah saith unto Isaac, 'I have been disgusted with my life because of the presence of the daughters of Heth; if Jacob take a wife of the daughters of Heth, like these -- from the daughters of the land -- why do I live?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Isaac calleth unto Jacob, and blesseth him, and commandeth him, and saith to him, 'Thou dost not take a wife of the daughters of Caanan;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: rise, go to Padan-Aram, to the house of Bethuel, thy mother's father, and take for thyself from thence a wife, of the daughters of Laban, thy mother's brother;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and God Almighty doth bless thee, and make thee fruitful, and multiply thee, and thou hast become an assembly of peoples;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and He doth give to thee the blessing of Abraham, to thee and to thy seed with thee, to cause thee to possess the land of thy sojournings, which God gave to Abraham.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Isaac sendeth away Jacob, and he goeth to Padan-Aram, unto Laban, son of Bethuel the Aramaean, brother of Rebekah, mother of Jacob and Esau.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Esau seeth that Isaac hath blessed Jacob, and hath sent him to Padan-Aram to take to himself from thence a wife -- in his blessing him that he layeth a charge upon him, saying, Thou dost not take a wife from the daughters of Canaan --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: that Jacob hearkeneth unto his father and unto his mother, and goeth to Padan-Aram --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Esau seeth that the daughters of Canaan are evil in the eyes of Isaac his father,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Esau goeth unto Ishmael, and taketh Mahalath, daughter of Ishmael, Abraham's son, sister of Nebajoth, unto his wives, to himself, for a wife.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jacob goeth out from Beer-Sheba, and goeth toward Haran,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he toucheth at a [certain] place, and lodgeth there, for the sun hath gone in, and he taketh of the stones of the place, and maketh [them] his pillows, and lieth down in that place.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he dreameth, and lo, a ladder set up on the earth, and its head is touching the heavens; and lo, messengers of God are going up and coming down by it;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and lo, Jehovah is standing upon it, and He saith, 'I [am] Jehovah, God of Abraham thy father, and God of Isaac; the land on which thou art lying, to thee I give it, and to thy seed;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and thy seed hath been as the dust of the land, and thou hast broken forth westward, and eastward, and northward, and southward, and all families of the ground have been blessed in thee and in thy seed.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And lo, I [am] with thee, and have kept thee whithersoever thou goest, and have caused thee to turn back unto this ground; for I leave thee not till that I have surely done that which I have spoken to thee.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jacob awaketh out of his sleep, and saith, 'Surely Jehovah is in this place, and I knew not;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he feareth, and saith, 'How fearful [is] this place; this is nothing but a house of God, and this a gate of the heavens.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jacob riseth early in the morning, and taketh the stone which he hath made his pillows, and maketh it a standing pillar, and poureth oil upon its top,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he calleth the name of that place Bethel, [house of God,] and yet, Luz [is] the name of the city at the first.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jacob voweth a vow, saying, 'Seeing God is with me, and hath kept me in this way which I am going, and hath given to me bread to eat, and a garment to put on --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: when I have turned back in peace unto the house of my father, and Jehovah hath become my God,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: then this stone which I have made a standing pillar is a house of God, and all that Thou dost give to me -- tithing I tithe to Thee.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jacob lifteth up his feet, and goeth towards the land of the sons of the east;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he looketh, and lo, a well in the field, and lo, there three droves of a flock crouching by it, for from that well they water the droves, and the great stone [is] on the mouth of the well.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: (When thither have all the droves been gathered, and they have rolled the stone from off the mouth of the well, and have watered the flock, then they have turned back the stone on the mouth of the well to its place.)

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jacob saith to them, 'My brethren, from whence [are] ye?' and they say, 'We [are] from Haran.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he saith to them, 'Have ye known Laban, son of Nahor?' and they say, 'We have known.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he saith to them, 'Hath he peace?' and they say, 'Peace; and lo, Rachel his daughter is coming with the flock.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he saith, 'Lo, the day [is] still great, [it is] not time for the cattle to be gathered; water ye the flock, and go, delight yourselves.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they say, 'We are not able, till that all the droves be gathered together, and they have rolled away the stone from the mouth of the well, and we have watered the flock.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He is yet speaking with them, and Rachel hath come with the flock which her father hath, for she [is] shepherdess;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and it cometh to pass when Jacob hath seen Rachel, daughter of Laban his mother's brother, and the flock of Laban his mother's brother, that Jacob cometh nigh and rolleth the stone from off the mouth of the well, and watereth the flock of Laban his mother's brother.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jacob kisseth Rachel, and lifteth up his voice, and weepeth,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
