translations.text: and Jacob declareth to Rachel that he [is] her father's brother, and that he [is] Rebekah's son, and she runneth and declareth to her father.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it cometh to pass, when Laban heareth the report of Jacob his sister's son, that he runneth to meet him, and embraceth him, and kisseth him, and bringeth him in unto his house; and he recounteth to Laban all these things,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Laban saith to him, 'Only my bone and my flesh [art] thou;' and he dwelleth with him a month of days.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Laban saith to Jacob, 'Is it because thou [art] my brother that thou hast served me for nought? declare to me what [is] thy hire.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Laban hath two daughters, the name of the elder [is] Leah, and the name of the younger Rachel,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the eyes of Leah [are] tender, and Rachel hath been fair of form and fair of appearance.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jacob loveth Rachel, and saith, 'I serve thee seven years for Rachel thy younger daughter:'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Laban saith, 'It is better for me to give her to thee than to give her to another man; dwell with me;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jacob serveth for Rachel seven years; and they are in his eyes as some days, because of his loving her.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jacob saith unto Laban, 'Give up my wife, for my days have been fulfilled, and I go in unto her;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Laban gathereth all the men of the place, and maketh a banquet.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it cometh to pass in the evening, that he taketh Leah, his daughter, and bringeth her in unto him, and he goeth in unto her;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Laban giveth to her Zilpah, his maid-servant, to Leah his daughter, a maid-servant.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it cometh to pass in the morning, that lo, it [is] Leah; and he saith unto Laban, 'What [is] this thou hast done to me? for Rachel have I not served with thee? and why hast thou deceived me?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Laban saith, 'It is not done so in our place, to give the younger before the first-born;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: fulfil the week of this one, and we give to thee also this one, for the service which thou dost serve with me yet seven other years.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jacob doth so, and fulfilleth the week of this one, and he giveth to him Rachel his daughter, to him for a wife;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Laban giveth to Rachel his daughter Bilhah his maid-servant, for a maid-servant to her.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he goeth in also unto Rachel, and he also loveth Rachel more than Leah; and he serveth with him yet seven other years.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah seeth that Leah [is] the hated one, and He openeth her womb, and Rachel [is] barren;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Leah conceiveth, and beareth a son, and calleth his name Reuben, for she said, 'Because Jehovah hath looked on mine affliction; because now doth my husband love me.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And she conceiveth again, and beareth a son, and saith, 'Because Jehovah hath heard that I [am] the hated one, He also giveth to me even this [one];' and she calleth his name Simeon.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And she conceiveth again, and beareth a son, and saith, 'Now [is] the time, my husband is joined unto me, because I have born to him three sons,' therefore hath [one] called his name Levi.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And she conceiveth again, and beareth a son, and saith this time, 'I praise Jehovah;' therefore hath she called his name Judah; and she ceaseth from bearing.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Rachel seeth that she hath not borne to Jacob, and Rachel is envious of her sister, and saith unto Jacob, 'Give me sons, and if there is none -- I die.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jacob's anger burneth against Rachel, and he saith, 'Am I in stead of God who hath withheld from thee the fruit of the womb?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And she saith, 'Lo, my handmaid Bilhah, go in unto her, and she doth bear on my knees, and I am built up, even I, from her;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and she giveth to him Bilhah her maid-servant for a wife, and Jacob goeth in unto her;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Bilhah conceiveth, and beareth to Jacob a son,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Rachel saith, 'God hath decided for me, and also hath hearkened to my voice, and giveth to me a son;' therefore hath she called his name Dan.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Bilhah, Rachel's maid-servant, conceiveth again, and beareth a second son to Jacob,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Rachel saith, 'With wrestlings of God I have wrestled with my sister, yea, I have prevailed;' and she calleth his name Napthali.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Leah seeth that she hath ceased from bearing, and she taketh Zilpah her maid-servant, and giveth her to Jacob for a wife;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Zilpah, Leah's maid-servant, beareth to Jacob a son,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Leah saith, 'A troop is coming;' and she calleth his name Gad.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Zilpah, Leah's maid-servant, beareth a second son to Jacob,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Leah saith, 'Because of my happiness, for daughters have pronounced me happy;' and she calleth his name Asher.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Reuben goeth in the days of wheat-harvest, and findeth love-apples in the field, and bringeth them in unto Leah, his mother, and Rachel saith unto Leah, 'Give to me, I pray thee, of the love-apples of thy son.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And she saith to her, 'Is thy taking my husband a little thing, that thou hast taken also the love-apples of my son?' and Rachel saith, 'Therefore doth he lie with thee to-night, for thy son's love-apples.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jacob cometh in from the field at evening; and Leah goeth to meet him, and saith, 'Unto me dost thou come in, for hiring I have hired thee with my son's love-apples;' and he lieth with her during that night.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And God hearkeneth unto Leah, and she conceiveth, and beareth to Jacob a son, a fifth,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Leah saith, 'God hath given my hire, because I have given my maid-servant to my husband;' and she calleth his name Issachar.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And conceive again doth Leah, and she beareth a sixth son to Jacob,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Leah saith, 'God hath endowed me -- a good dowry; this time doth my husband dwell with me, for I have borne to him six sons;' and she calleth his name Zebulun;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and afterwards hath she born a daughter, and calleth her name Dinah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And God remembereth Rachel, and God hearkeneth unto her, and openeth her womb,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and she conceiveth and beareth a son, and saith, 'God hath gathered up my reproach;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and she calleth his name Joseph, saying, 'Jehovah is adding to me another son.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it cometh to pass, when Rachel hath borne Joseph, that Jacob saith unto Laban, 'Send me away, and I go unto my place, and to my land;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: give up my wives and my children, for whom I have served thee, and I go; for thou -- thou hast known my service which I have served thee.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Laban saith unto him, 'If, I pray thee, I have found grace in thine eyes -- I have observed diligently that Jehovah doth bless me for thy sake.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He saith also, 'Define thy hire to me, and I give.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he saith unto him, 'Thou -- thou hast known that which I have served thee [in], and that which thy substance was with me;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for [it is] little which thou hast had at my appearance, and it breaketh forth into a multitude, and Jehovah blesseth thee at my coming; and now, when do I make, I also, for mine own house?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he saith, 'What do I give to thee?' And Jacob saith, 'Thou dost not give me anything; if thou do for me this thing, I turn back; I have delight; thy flock I watch;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I pass through all thy flock to-day to turn aside from thence every sheep speckled and spotted, and every brown sheep among the lambs, and speckled and spotted among the goats -- and it hath been my hire;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and my righteousness hath answered for me in the day to come, when it cometh in for my hire before thy face; -- every one which is not speckled and spotted among [my] goats, and brown among [my] lambs -- it is stolen with me.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Laban saith, 'Lo, O that it were according to thy word;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he turneth aside during that day the ring-streaked and the spotted he-goats, and all the speckled and the spotted she-goats, every one that [hath] white in it, and every brown one among the lambs, and he giveth into the hand of his sons,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and setteth a journey of three days between himself and Jacob; and Jacob is feeding the rest of the flock of Laban.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jacob taketh to himself a rod of fresh poplar, and of the hazel and chesnut, and doth peel in them white peelings, making bare the white that [is] on the rods,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and setteth up the rods which he hath peeled in the gutters in the watering troughs (when the flock cometh in to drink), over-against the flock, that they may conceive in their coming in to drink;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the flocks conceive at the rods, and the flock beareth ring-streaked, speckled, and spotted ones.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the lambs hath Jacob parted, and he putteth the face of the flock towards the ring-streaked, also all the brown in the flock of Laban, and he setteth his own droves by themselves, and hath not set them near Laban's flock.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it hath come to pass whenever the strong ones of the flock conceive, that Jacob set the rods before the eyes of the flock in the gutters, to cause them to conceive by the rods,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and when the flock is feeble, he doth not set [them]; and the feeble ones have been Laban's, and the strong ones Jacob's.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the man increaseth very exceedingly, and hath many flocks, and maid-servants, and men-servants, and camels, and asses.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he heareth the words of Laban's sons, saying, 'Jacob hath taken all that our father hath; yea, from that which our father hath, he hath made all this honour;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jacob seeth the face of Laban, and lo, it is not with him as heretofore.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah saith unto Jacob, 'Turn back unto the land of thy fathers, and to thy kindred, and I am with thee.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jacob sendeth and calleth for Rachel and for Leah to the field unto his flock;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and saith to them, 'I am beholding your father's face -- that it is not towards me as heretofore, and the God of my father hath been with me,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and ye -- ye have known that with all my power I have served your father,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and your father hath played upon me, and hath changed my hire ten times; and God hath not suffered him to do evil with me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'If he say thus: The speckled are thy hire, then bare all the flock speckled ones; and if he say thus: The ring-streaked are thy hire, then bare all the flock ring-streaked;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and God taketh away the substance of your father, and doth give to me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And it cometh to pass at the time of the flock conceiving, that I lift up mine eyes and see in a dream, and lo, the he-goats, which are going up on the flock, [are] ring-streaked, speckled, and grisled;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the messenger of God saith unto me in the dream, Jacob, and I say, Here [am] I.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And He saith, Lift up, I pray thee, thine eyes, and see -- all the he-goats which are going up on the flock [are] ring-streaked, speckled, and grisled, for I have seen all that Laban is doing to thee;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I [am] the God of Bethel where thou hast anointed a standing pillar, where thou hast vowed a vow to me; now, arise, go out from this land, and turn back unto the land of thy birth.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Rachel answereth -- Leah also -- and saith to him, 'Have we yet a portion and inheritance in the house of our father?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: have we not been reckoned strangers to him? for he hath sold us, and he also utterly consumeth our money;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for all the wealth which God hath taken away from our father, it [is] ours, and our children's; and now, all that God hath said unto thee -- do.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jacob riseth, and lifteth up his sons and his wives on the camels,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and leadeth all his cattle, and all his substance which he hath acquired, the cattle of his getting, which he hath acquired in Padan-Aram, to go unto Isaac his father, to the land of Canaan.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Laban hath gone to shear his flock, and Rachel stealeth the teraphim which her father hath;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jacob deceiveth the heart of Laban the Aramaean, because he hath not declared to him that he is fleeing;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he fleeth, he and all that he hath, and riseth, and passeth over the River, and setteth his face [toward] the mount of Gilead.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it is told to Laban on the third day that Jacob hath fled,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he taketh his brethren with him, and pursueth after him a journey of seven days, and overtaketh him in the mount of Gilead.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And God cometh in unto Laban the Aramaean in a dream of the night, and saith to him, 'Take heed to thyself lest thou speak with Jacob from good unto evil.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Laban overtaketh Jacob; and Jacob hath fixed his tent in the mount; and Laban with his brethren have fixed [theirs] in the mount of Gilead.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Laban saith to Jacob, 'What hast thou done that thou dost deceive my heart, and lead away my daughters as captives of the sword?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Why hast thou hidden thyself to flee, and deceivest me, and hast not declared to me, and I send thee away with joy and with songs, with tabret and with harp,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and hast not suffered me to kiss my sons and my daughters? -- now thou hast acted foolishly in doing [so];

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: my hand is to God to do evil with you, but the God of your father yesternight hath spoken unto me, saying, Take heed to thyself from speaking with Jacob from good unto evil.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And now, thou hast certainly gone, because thou hast been very desirous for the house of thy father; why hast thou stolen my gods?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jacob answereth and saith to Laban, 'Because I was afraid, for I said, Lest thou take violently away thy daughters from me;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: with whomsoever thou findest thy gods -- he doth not live; before our brethren discern for thyself what [is] with me, and take to thyself:' and Jacob hath not known that Rachel hath stolen them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Laban goeth into the tent of Jacob, and into the tent of Leah, and into the tent of the two handmaidens, and hath not found; and he goeth out from the tent of Leah, and goeth into the tent of Rachel.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Rachel hath taken the teraphim, and putteth them in the furniture of the camel, and sitteth upon them; and Laban feeleth all the tent, and hath not found;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and she saith unto her father, 'Let it not be displeasing in the eyes of my lord that I am not able to rise at thy presence, for the way of women [is] on me;' and he searcheth, and hath not found the teraphim.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it is displeasing to Jacob, and he striveth with Laban; and Jacob answereth and saith to Laban, 'What [is] my transgression? what my sin, that thou hast burned after me?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for thou hast felt all my vessels: what hast thou found of all the vessels of thy house? set here before my brethren, and thy brethren, and they decide between us both.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'These twenty years I [am] with thee: thy ewes and thy she-goats have not miscarried, and the rams of thy flock I have not eaten;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: the torn I have not brought in unto thee -- I, I repay it -- from my hand thou dost seek it; I have been deceived by day, and I have been deceived by night;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I have been [thus]: in the day consumed me hath drought, and frost by night, and wander doth my sleep from mine eyes.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'This [is] to me twenty years in thy house: I have served thee fourteen years for thy two daughters, and six years for thy flock; and thou changest my hire ten times;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: unless the God of my father, the God of Abraham, and the Fear of Isaac, had been for me, surely now empty thou hadst sent me away; mine affliction and the labour of my hands hath God seen, and reproveth yesternight.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Laban answereth and saith unto Jacob, 'The daughters [are] my daughters, and the sons my sons, and the flock my flock, and all that thou art seeing [is] mine; and to my daughters -- what do I to these to-day, or to their sons whom they have born?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and now, come, let us make a covenant, I and thou, and it hath been for a witness between me and thee.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jacob taketh a stone, and lifteth it up [for] a standing pillar;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jacob saith to his brethren, 'Gather stones,' and they take stones, and make a heap; and they eat there on the heap;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Laban calleth it Jegar-Sahadutha; and Jacob hath called it Galeed.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Laban saith, 'This heap [is] witness between me and thee to-day;' therefore hath he called its name Galeed;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Mizpah also, for he said, 'Jehovah doth watch between me and thee, for we are hidden one from another;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: if thou afflict my daughters, or take wives beside my daughters -- there is no man with us -- see, God [is] witness between me and thee.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Laban saith to Jacob, 'Lo, this heap, and lo, the standing pillar which I have cast between me and thee;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: this heap [is] witness, and the standing pillar [is] witness, that I do not pass over this heap unto thee, and that thou dost not pass over this heap and this standing pillar unto me -- for evil;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: the God of Abraham and the God of Nahor, doth judge between us -- the God of their father,' and Jacob sweareth by the Fear of his father Isaac.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jacob sacrificeth a sacrifice in the mount, and calleth to his brethren to eat bread, and they eat bread, and lodge in the mount;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Laban riseth early in the morning, and kisseth his sons and his daughters, and blesseth them; and Laban goeth on, and turneth back to his place.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jacob hath gone on his way, and messengers of God come upon him;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jacob saith, when he hath seen them, 'This [is] the camp of God;' and he calleth the name of that place 'Two Camps.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jacob sendeth messengers before him unto Esau his brother, towards the land of Seir, the field of Edom,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and commandeth them, saying, 'Thus do ye say to my lord, to Esau: Thus said thy servant Jacob, With Laban I have sojourned, and I tarry until now;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and I have ox, and ass, flock, and man-servant, and maid-servant, and I send to declare to my lord, to find grace in his eyes.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the messengers turn back unto Jacob, saying, 'We came in unto thy brother, unto Esau, and he also is coming to meet thee, and four hundred men with him;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jacob feareth exceedingly, and is distressed, and he divideth the people who [are] with him, and the flock, and the herd, and the camels, into two camps,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and saith, 'If Esau come in unto the one camp, and have smitten it -- then the camp which is left hath been for an escape.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jacob saith, 'God of my father Abraham, and God of my father Isaac, Jehovah who saith unto me, Turn back to thy land, and to thy kindred, and I do good with thee:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I have been unworthy of all the kind acts, and of all the truth which Thou hast done with thy servant -- for, with my staff I passed over this Jordan, and now I have become two camps.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Deliver me, I pray Thee, from the hand of my brother, from the hand of Esau: for I am fearing him, less he come and have smitten me -- mother beside sons;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Thou -- Thou hast said, I certainly do good with thee, and have set thy seed as the sand of the sea, which is not numbered because of the multitude.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he lodgeth there during that night, and taketh from that which is coming into his hand, a present for Esau his brother:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: she-goats two hundred, and he-goats twenty, ewes two hundred, and rams twenty,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: suckling camels and their young ones thirty, cows forty, and bullocks ten, she-asses twenty, and foals ten;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he giveth into the hand of his servants, every drove by itself, and saith unto his servants, 'Pass over before me, and a space ye do put between drove and drove.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he commandeth the first, saying, 'When Esau my brother meeteth thee, and hath asked thee, saying, Whose [art] thou? and whither goest thou? and whose [are] these before thee?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: then thou hast said, Thy servant Jacob's: it [is] a present sent to my lord, to Esau; and lo, he also [is] behind us.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he commandeth also the second, also the third, also all who are going after the droves, saying, 'According to this manner do ye speak unto Esau in your finding him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and ye have said also, Lo, thy servant Jacob [is] behind us;' for he said, 'I pacify his face with the present which is going before me, and afterwards I see his face; it may be he lifteth up my face;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the present passeth over before his face, and he hath lodged during that night in the camp.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he riseth in that night, and taketh his two wives, and his two maid-servants, and his eleven children, and passeth over the passage of Jabbok;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he taketh them, and causeth them to pass over the brook, and he causeth that which he hath to pass over.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jacob is left alone, and one wrestleth with him till the ascending of the dawn;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he seeth that he is not able for him, and he cometh against the hollow of his thigh, and the hollow of Jacob's thigh is disjointed in his wrestling with him;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he saith, 'Send me away, for the dawn hath ascended:' and he saith, 'I send thee not away, except thou hast blessed me.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he saith unto him, 'What [is] thy name?' and he saith, 'Jacob.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he saith, 'Thy name is no more called Jacob, but Israel; for thou hast been a prince with God and with men, and dost prevail.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jacob asketh, and saith, 'Declare, I pray thee, thy name;' and he saith, 'Why [is] this, thou askest for My name?' and He blesseth him there.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jacob calleth the name of the place Peniel: for 'I have seen God face unto face, and my life is delivered;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the sun riseth on him when he hath passed over Penuel, and he is halting on his thigh;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: therefore the sons of Israel do not eat the sinew which shrank, which [is] on the hollow of the thigh, unto this day, because He came against the hollow of Jacob's thigh, against the sinew which shrank.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jacob lifteth up his eyes, and looketh, and lo, Esau is coming, and with him four hundred men; and he divideth the children unto Leah, and unto Rachel, and unto the two maid-servants;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he setteth the maid-servants and their children first, and Leah and her children behind, and Rachel and Joseph last.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he himself passed over before them, and boweth himself to the earth seven times, until his drawing nigh unto his brother,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Esau runneth to meet him, and embraceth him, and falleth on his neck, and kisseth him, and they weep;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he lifteth up his eyes, and seeth the women and the children, and saith, 'What [are] these to thee?' And he saith, 'The children with whom God hath favoured thy servant.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the maid-servants draw nigh, they and their children, and bow themselves;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Leah also draweth nigh, and her children, and they bow themselves; and afterwards Joseph hath drawn nigh with Rachel, and they bow themselves.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he saith, 'What to thee [is] all this camp which I have met?' and he saith, 'To find grace in the eyes of my lord.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Esau saith, 'I have abundance, my brother, let it be to thyself that which thou hast.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jacob saith, 'Nay, I pray thee, if, I pray thee, I have found grace in thine eyes, then thou hast received my present from my hand, because that I have seen thy face, as the seeing of the face of God, and thou art pleased with me;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: receive, I pray thee, my blessing, which is brought to thee, because God hath favoured me, and because I have all [things];' and he presseth on him, and he receiveth,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and saith, 'Let us journey and go on, and I go on before thee.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he saith unto him, 'My lord knoweth that the children [are] tender, and the suckling flock and the herd [are] with me; when they have beaten them one day, then hath all the flock died.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Let my lord, I pray thee, pass over before his servant, and I -- I lead on gently, according to the foot of the work which [is] before me, and to the foot of the children, until that I come unto my lord, to Seir.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Esau saith, 'Let me, I pray thee, place with thee some of the people who [are] with me;' and he said, 'Why [is] this? I find grace in the eyes of my lord.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And turn back on that day doth Esau on his way to Seir;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jacob hath journeyed to Succoth, and buildeth to himself a house, and for his cattle hath made booths, therefore hath he called the name of the place Succoth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jacob cometh in to Shalem, a city of Shechem, which [is] in the land of Canaan, in his coming from Padan-Aram, and encampeth before the city,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he buyeth the portion of the field where he hath stretched out his tent, from the hand of the sons of Hamor, father of Shechem, for a hundred kesitah;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he setteth up there an altar, and proclaimeth at it God -- the God of Israel.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Dinah, daughter of Leah, whom she hath borne to Jacob, goeth out to look on the daughters of the land,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Shechem, son of Hamor the Hivite, a prince of the land, seeth her, and taketh her, and lieth with her, and humbleth her;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and his soul cleaveth to Dinah, daughter of Jacob, and he loveth the young person, and speaketh unto the heart of the young person.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Shechem speaketh unto Hamor his father, saying, 'Take for me this damsel for a wife.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jacob hath heard that he hath defiled Dinah his daughter, and his sons were with his cattle in the field, and Jacob kept silent till their coming.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Hamor, father of Shechem, goeth out unto Jacob to speak with him;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the sons of Jacob came in from the field when they heard, and the men grieve themselves, and it [is] very displeasing to them, for folly he hath done against Israel, to lie with the daughter of Jacob -- and so it is not done.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Hamor speaketh with them, saying, 'Shechem, my son, his soul hath cleaved to your daughter; give her, I pray you, to him for a wife,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and join ye in marriage with us; your daughters ye give to us, and our daughters ye take to yourselves,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and with us ye dwell, and the land is before you; dwell ye and trade [in] it, and have possessions in it.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Shechem saith unto her father, and unto her brethren, 'Let me find grace in your eyes, and that which ye say unto me, I give;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: multiply on me exceedingly dowry and gift, and I give as ye say unto me, and give to me the young person for a wife.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the sons of Jacob answer Shechem and Hamor his father deceitfully, and they speak (because he defiled Dinah their sister),

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and say unto them, 'We are not able to do this thing, to give our sister to one who hath a foreskin: for it [is] a reproach to us.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Only for this we consent to you; if ye be as we, to have every male of you circumcised,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: then we have given our daughters to you, and your daughters we take to ourselves, and we have dwelt with you, and have become one people;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and if ye hearken not unto us to be circumcised, then we have taken our daughter, and have gone.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And their words are good in the eyes of Hamor, and in the eyes of Shechem, Hamor's son;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the young man delayed not to do the thing, for he had delight in Jacob's daughter, and he is honourable above all the house of his father.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Hamor cometh -- Shechem his son also -- unto the gate of their city, and they speak unto the men of their city, saying,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'These men are peaceable with us; then let them dwell in the land, and trade [in] it; and the land, lo, [is] wide before them; their daughters let us take to ourselves for wives, and our daughters give to them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Only for this do the men consent to us, to dwell with us, to become one people, in every male of us being circumcised, as they are circumcised;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: their cattle, and their substance, and all their beasts -- are they not ours? only let us consent to them, and they dwell with us.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And unto Hamor, and unto Shechem his son, hearken do all those going out of the gate of his city, and every male is circumcised, all those going out of the gate of his city.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it cometh to pass, on the third day, in their being pained, that two of the sons of Jacob, Simeon and Levi, Dinah's brethren, take each his sword, and come in against the city confidently, and slay every male;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Hamor, and Shechem his son, they have slain by the mouth of the sword, and they take Dinah out of Shechem's house, and go out.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Jacob's sons have come in upon the wounded, and they spoil the city, because they had defiled their sister;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: their flock and their herd, and their asses, and that which [is] in the city, and that which [is] in the field, have they taken;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and all their wealth, and all their infants, and their wives they have taken captive, and they spoil also all that [is] in the house.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jacob saith unto Simeon and unto Levi, 'Ye have troubled me, by causing me to stink among the inhabitants of the land, among the Canaanite, and among the Perizzite: and I [am] few in number, and they have been gathered against me, and have smitten me, and I have been destroyed, I and my house.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they say, 'As a harlot doth he make our sister?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And God saith unto Jacob, 'Rise, go up to Bethel, and dwell there, and make there an altar to God, who appeared unto thee in thy fleeing from the face of Esau thy brother.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jacob saith unto his household, and unto all who [are] with him, 'Turn aside the gods of the stranger which [are] in your midst, and cleanse yourselves, and change your garments;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and we rise, and go up to Bethel, and I make there an altar to God, who is answering me in the day of my distress, and is with me in the way that I have gone.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they give unto Jacob all the gods of the stranger that [are] in their hand, and the rings that [are] in their ears, and Jacob hideth them under the oak which [is] by Shechem;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they journey, and the terror of God is on the cities which [are] round about them, and they have not pursued after the sons of Jacob.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jacob cometh in to Luz which [is] in the land of Canaan (it [is] Bethel), he and all the people who [are] with him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he buildeth there an altar, and proclaimeth at the place the God of Bethel: for there had God been revealed unto him, in his fleeing from the face of his brother.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Deborah, Rebekah's nurse, dieth, and she is buried at the lower part of Bethel, under the oak, and he calleth its name 'Oak of weeping.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And God appeareth unto Jacob again, in his coming from Padan-Aram, and blesseth him;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and God saith to him, 'Thy name [is] Jacob: thy name is no more called Jacob, but Israel is thy name;' and He calleth his name Israel.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And God saith to him, 'I [am] God Almighty; be fruitful and multiply, a nation and an assembly of nations is from thee, and kings from thy loins go out;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the land which I have given to Abraham and to Isaac -- to thee I give it, yea to thy seed after thee I give the land.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And God goeth up from him, in the place where He hath spoken with him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jacob setteth up a standing pillar in the place where He hath spoken with him, a standing pillar of stone, and he poureth on it an oblation, and he poureth on it oil;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jacob calleth the name of the place where God spake with him Bethel.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they journey from Bethel, and there is yet a kibrath of land before entering Ephratha, and Rachel beareth, and is sharply pained in her bearing;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and it cometh to pass, in her being sharply pained in her bearing, that the midwife saith to her, 'Fear not, for this also [is] a son for thee.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it cometh to pass in the going out of her soul (for she died), that she calleth his name Ben-Oni; and his father called him Benjamin;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Rachel dieth, and is buried in the way to Ephratha, which [is] Bethlehem,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jacob setteth up a standing pillar over her grave; which [is] the standing pillar of Rachel's grave unto this day.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Israel journeyeth, and stretcheth out his tent beyond the tower of Edar;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and it cometh to pass in Israel's dwelling in that land, that Reuben goeth, and lieth with Bilhah his father's concubine; and Israel heareth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the sons of Jacob are twelve. Sons of Leah: Jacob's first-born Reuben, and Simeon, and Levi, and Judah, and Issachar, and Zebulun.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Sons of Rachel: Joseph and Benjamin.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And sons of Bilhah, Rachel's maid-servant: Dan and Naphtali.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And sons of Zilpah, Leah's maid-servant: Gad and Asher. These [are] sons of Jacob, who have been born to him in Padan-Aram.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jacob cometh unto Isaac his father, at Mamre, the city of Arba (which [is] Hebron), where Abraham and Isaac have sojourned.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the days of Isaac are a hundred and eighty years,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Isaac expireth, and dieth, and is gathered unto his people, aged and satisfied with days; and bury him do Esau and Jacob his sons.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And these [are] births of Esau, who [is] Edom.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Esau hath taken his wives from the daughters of Canaan: Adah daughter of Elon the Hittite, and Aholibamah daughter of Anah, daughter of Zibeon the Hivite,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Bashemath daughter of Ishmael, sister of Nebajoth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Adah beareth to Esau, Eliphaz; and Bashemath hath born Reuel;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Aholibamah hath born Jeush, and Jaalam, and Korah. These [are] sons of Esau, who were born to him in the land of Canaan.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Esau taketh his wives, and his sons, and his daughters, and all the persons of his house, and his cattle, and all his beasts, and all his substance which he hath acquired in the land of Canaan, and goeth into the country from the face of Jacob his brother;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for their substance was more abundant than to dwell together, and the land of their sojournings was not able to bear them because of their cattle;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Esau dwelleth in mount Seir: Esau is Edom.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And these [are] births of Esau, father of Edom, in mount Seir.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: These [are] the names of the sons of Esau: Eliphaz son of Adah, wife of Esau; Reuel son of Bashemath, wife of Esau.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the sons of Eliphaz are Teman, Omar, Zepho, and Gatam, and Kenaz;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Timnath hath been concubine to Eliphaz son of Esau, and she beareth to Eliphaz, Amalek; these [are] sons of Adah wife of Esau.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And these [are] sons of Reuel: Nahath and Zerah, Shammah and Mizzah; these were sons of Bashemath wife of Esau.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And these have been the sons of Aholibamah daughter of Anah, daughter of Zibeon, wife of Esau; and she beareth to Esau, Jeush and Jaalam and Korah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: These [are] chiefs of the sons of Esau: sons of Eliphaz, first-born of Esau: chief Teman, chief Omar, chief Zepho, chief Kenaz,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: chief Korah, chief Gatam, chief Amalek; these [are] chiefs of Eliphaz, in the land of Edom; these [are] sons of Adah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And these [are] sons of Reuel son of Esau: chief Nahath, chief Zerah, chief Shammah, chief Mizzah; these [are] chiefs of Reuel, in the land of Edom; these [are] sons of Bashemath wife of Esau.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And these [are] sons of Aholibamah wife of Esau: chief Jeush, chief Jaalam, chief Korah; these [are] chiefs of Aholibamah daughter of Anah, wife of Esau.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: These [are] sons of Esau (who [is] Edom), and these their chiefs.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: These [are] sons of Seir the Horite, the inhabitants of the land: Lotan, and Shobal, and Zibeon, and Anah,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Dishon, and Ezer, and Dishan; these [are] chiefs of the Horites, sons of Seir, in the land of Edom.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the sons of Lotan are Hori and Heman; and a sister of Lotan [is] Timna.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And these [are] sons of Shobal: Alvan and Manahath, and Ebal, Shepho and Onam.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And these [are] sons of Zibeon, both Ajah and Anah: it [is] Anah that hath found the Imim in the wilderness, in his feeding the asses of Zibeon his father.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And these [are] sons of Anah: Dishon, and Aholibamah daughter of Anah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And these [are] sons of Dishon: Hemdan, and Eshban, and Ithran, and Cheran.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: These [are] sons of Ezer: Bilhan, and Zaavan, and Akan.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: These [are] sons of Dishan: Uz and Aran.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: These [are] chiefs of the Horite: chief Lotan, chief Shobal, chief Zibeon, chief Anah,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: chief Dishon, chief Ezer, chief Dishan: these [are] chiefs of the Horite in reference to their chiefs in the land of Seir.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And these [are] the kings who have reigned in the land of Edom before the reigning of a king over the sons of Israel.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Bela son of Beor reigneth in Edom, and the name of his city [is] Dinhabah;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Bela dieth, and reign in his stead doth Jobab son of Zerah from Bozrah;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jobab dieth, and reign in his stead doth Husham from the land of the Temanite.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Husham dieth, and reign in his stead doth Hadad son of Bedad (who smiteth Midian in the field of Moab), and the name of his city [is] Avith;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Hadad dieth, and reign in his stead doth Samlah of Masrekah;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Samlah dieth, and reign in his stead doth Saul from Rehoboth of the River;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Saul dieth, and reign in his stead doth Baal-hanan son of Achbor;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Baal-hanan son of Achbor dieth, and reign in his stead doth Hadar, and the name of his city [is] Pau; and his wife's name [is] Mehetabel daughter of Matred, daughter of Me-zahab.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And these [are] the names of the chiefs of Esau, according to their families, according to their places, by their names: chief Timnah, chief Alvah, chief Jetheth,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: chief Aholibamah, chief Elah, chief Pinon,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: chief Kenaz, chief Teman, chief Mibzar,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: chief Magdiel, chief Iram: these [are] chiefs of Edom, in reference to their dwellings, in the land of their possession; he [is] Esau father of Edom.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jacob dwelleth in the land of his father's sojournings -- in the land of Canaan.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: These [are] births of Jacob: Joseph, a son of seventeen years, hath been enjoying himself with his brethren among the flock, (and he [is] a youth,) with the sons of Bilhah, and with the sons of Zilpah, his father's wives, and Joseph bringeth in an account of their evil unto their father.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Israel hath loved Joseph more than any of his sons, for he [is] a son of his old age, and hath made for him a long coat;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and his brethren see that their father hath loved him more than any of his brethren, and they hate him, and have not been able to speak [to] him peaceably.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Joseph dreameth a dream, and declareth to his brethren, and they add still more to hate him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he saith unto them, 'Hear ye, I pray you, this dream which I have dreamed:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: that, lo, we are binding bundles in the midst of the field, and lo, my bundle hath arisen, and hath also stood up, and lo, your bundles are round about, and bow themselves to my bundle.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And his brethren say to him, 'Dost thou certainly reign over us? dost thou certainly rule over us?' and they add still more to hate him, for his dreams, and for his words.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he dreameth yet another dream, and recounteth it to his brethren, and saith, 'Lo, I have dreamed a dream again, and lo, the sun and the moon, and eleven stars, are bowing themselves to me.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he recounteth unto his father, and unto his brethren; and his father pusheth against him, and saith to him, 'What [is] this dream which thou hast dreamt? do we certainly come -- I, and thy mother, and thy brethren -- to bow ourselves to thee, to the earth?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and his brethren are zealous against him, and his father hath watched the matter.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And his brethren go to feed the flock of their father in Shechem,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Israel saith unto Joseph, 'Are not thy brethren feeding in Shechem? come, and I send thee unto them;' and he saith to him, 'Here [am] I;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he saith to him, 'Go, I pray thee, see the peace of thy brethren, and the peace of the flock, and bring me back word;' and he sendeth him from the valley of Hebron, and he cometh to Shechem.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And a man findeth him, and lo, he is wandering in the field, and the man asketh him, saying, 'What seekest thou?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he saith, 'My brethren I am seeking, declare to me, I pray thee, where they are feeding?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the man saith, 'They have journeyed from this, for I have heard some saying, Let us go to Dothan,' and Joseph goeth after his brethren, and findeth them in Dothan.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they see him from afar, even before he draweth near unto them, and they conspire against him to put him to death.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they say one unto another, 'Lo, this man of the dreams cometh;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and now, come, and we slay him, and cast him into one of the pits, and have said, An evil beast hath devoured him; and we see what his dreams are.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Reuben heareth, and delivereth him out of their hand, and saith, 'Let us not smite the life;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Reuben saith unto them, 'Shed no blood; cast him into this pit which [is] in the wilderness, and put not forth a hand upon him,' -- in order to deliver him out of their hand, to bring him back unto his father.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it cometh to pass, when Joseph hath come unto his brethren, that they strip Joseph of his coat, the long coat which [is] upon him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and take him and cast him into the pit, and the pit [is] empty, there is no water in it.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they sit down to eat bread, and they lift up their eyes, and look, and lo, a company of Ishmaelites coming from Gilead, and their camels bearing spices, and balm, and myrrh, going to take [them] down to Egypt.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Judah saith unto his brethren, 'What gain when we slay our brother, and have concealed his blood?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Come, and we sell him to the Ishmaelites, and our hands are not on him, for he [is] our brother -- our flesh;' and his brethren hearken.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Midianite merchantmen pass by and they draw out and bring up Joseph out of the pit, and sell Joseph to the Ishmaelites for twenty silverlings, and they bring Joseph into Egypt.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Reuben returneth unto the pit, and lo, Joseph is not in the pit, and he rendeth his garments,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he returneth unto his brethren, and saith, 'The lad is not, and I -- whither am I going?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they take the coat of Joseph, and slaughter a kid of the goats, and dip the coat in the blood,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and send the long coat, and they bring [it] in unto their father, and say, 'This have we found; discern, we pray thee, whether it [is] thy son's coat or not?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he discerneth it, and saith, 'My son's coat! an evil beast hath devoured him; torn -- torn is Joseph!'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jacob rendeth his raiment, and putteth sackcloth on his loins, and becometh a mourner for his son many days,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and all his sons and all his daughters rise to comfort him, and he refuseth to comfort himself, and saith, 'For -- I go down mourning unto my son, to Sheol,' and his father weepeth for him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the Medanites have sold him unto Egypt, to Potiphar, a eunuch of Pharaoh, head of the executioners.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it cometh to pass, at that time, that Judah goeth down from his brethren, and turneth aside unto a man, an Adullamite, whose name [is] Hirah;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Judah seeth there the daughter of a man, a Canaanite, whose name [is] Shuah, and taketh her, and goeth in unto her.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And she conceiveth, and beareth a son, and he calleth his name Er;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and she conceiveth again, and beareth a son, and calleth his name Onan;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and she addeth again, and beareth a son, and calleth his name Shelah; and he was in Chezib in her bearing him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Judah taketh a wife for Er, his first-born, and her name [is] Tamar;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Er, Judah's first-born, is evil in the eyes of Jehovah, and Jehovah doth put him to death.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Judah saith to Onan, 'Go in unto the wife of thy brother, and marry her, and raise up seed to thy brother;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Onan knoweth that the seed is not [reckoned] his; and it hath come to pass, if he hath gone in unto his brother's wife, that he hath destroyed [it] to the earth, so as not to give seed to his brother;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and that which he hath done is evil in the eyes of Jehovah, and He putteth him also to death.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Judah saith to Tamar his daughter-in-law, 'Abide a widow at thy father's house, till Shelah my son groweth up;' for he said, 'Lest he die -- even he -- like his brethren;' and Tamar goeth and dwelleth at her father's house.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the days are multiplied, and the daughter of Shuah, Judah's wife, dieth; and Judah is comforted, and goeth up unto his sheep-shearers, he and Hirah his friend the Adullamite, to Timnath.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it is declared to Tamar, saying, 'Lo, thy husband's father is going up to Timnath to shear his flock;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and she turneth aside the garments of her widowhood from off her, and covereth herself with a vail, and wrappeth herself up, and sitteth in the opening of Enayim, which [is] by the way to Timnath, for she hath seen that Shelah hath grown up, and she hath not been given to him for a wife.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Judah seeth her, and reckoneth her for a harlot, for she hath covered her face,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he turneth aside unto her by the way, and saith, 'Come, I pray thee, let me come in unto thee,' (for he hath not known that she [is] his daughter-in-law); and she saith, 'What dost thou give to me, that thou mayest come in unto me?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he saith, 'I -- I send a kid of the goats from the flock.' And she saith, 'Dost thou give a pledge till thou send [it]?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he saith, 'What [is] the pledge that I give to thee?' and she saith, 'Thy seal, and thy ribbon, and thy staff which [is] in thy hand;' and he giveth to her, and goeth in unto her, and she conceiveth to him;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and she riseth, and goeth, and turneth aside her vail from off her, and putteth on the garments of her widowhood.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Judah sendeth the kid of the goats by the hand of his friend the Adullamite, to receive the pledge from the hand of the woman, and he hath not found her.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he asketh the men of her place, saying, 'Where [is] the separated one -- she in Enayim, by the way?' and they say, 'There hath not been in this [place] a separated one.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he turneth back unto Judah, and saith, 'I have not found her; and the men of the place also have said, There hath not been in this [place] a separated one,'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Judah saith, 'Let her take to herself, lest we become despised; lo, I sent this kid, and thou hast not found her.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it cometh to pass about three months [after], that it is declared to Judah, saying, 'Tamar thy daughter-in-law hath committed fornication; and also, lo, she hath conceived by fornication:' and Judah saith, 'Bring her out -- and she is burnt.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: She is brought out, and she hath sent unto her husband's father, saying, 'To a man whose these [are], I [am] pregnant;' and she saith, 'Discern, I pray thee, whose [are] these -- the seal, and the ribbons, and the staff.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Judah discerneth and saith, 'She hath been more righteous than I, because that I did not give her to Shelah my son;' and he hath not added to know her again.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it cometh to pass in the time of her bearing, that lo, twins [are] in her womb;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and it cometh to pass in her bearing, that [one] giveth out a hand, and the midwife taketh and bindeth on his hand a scarlet thread, saying, 'This hath come out first.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it cometh to pass as he draweth back his hand, that lo, his brother hath come out, and she saith, 'What! thou hast broken forth -- on thee [is] the breach;' and he calleth his name Pharez;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and afterwards hath his brother come out, on whose hand [is] the scarlet thread, and he calleth his name Zarah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Joseph hath been brought down to Egypt, and Potiphar, a eunuch of Pharaoh, head of the executioners, an Egyptian man, buyeth him out of the hands of the Ishmaelites who have brought him thither.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah is with Joseph, and he is a prosperous man, and he is in the house of his lord the Egyptian,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and his lord seeth that Jehovah is with him, and all that he is doing Jehovah is causing to prosper in his hand,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Joseph findeth grace in his eyes and serveth him, and he appointeth him over his house, and all that he hath he hath given into his hand.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it cometh to pass from the time that he hath appointed him over his house, and over all that he hath, that Jehovah blesseth the house of the Egyptian for Joseph's sake, and the blessing of Jehovah is on all that he hath, in the house, and in the field;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he leaveth all that he hath in the hand of Joseph, and he hath not known anything that he hath, except the bread which he is eating. And Joseph is of a fair form, and of a fair appearance.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it cometh to pass after these things, that his lord's wife lifteth up her eyes unto Joseph, and saith, 'Lie with me;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he refuseth, and saith unto his lord's wife, 'Lo, my lord hath not known what [is] with me in the house, and all that he hath he hath given into my hand;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: none is greater in this house than I, and he hath not withheld from me anything, except thee, because thou [art] his wife; and how shall I do this great evil? -- then have I sinned against God.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it cometh to pass at her speaking unto Joseph day [by] day, that he hath not hearkened unto her, to lie near her, to be with her;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and it cometh to pass about this day, that he goeth into the house to do his work, and there is none of the men of the house there in the house,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and she catcheth him by his garment, saying, 'Lie with me;' and he leaveth his garment in her hand, and fleeth, and goeth without.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it cometh to pass when she seeth that he hath left his garment in her hand, and fleeth without,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: that she calleth for the men of her house, and speaketh to them, saying, 'See, he hath brought in to us a man, a Hebrew, to play with us; he hath come in unto me, to lie with me, and I call with a loud voice,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and it cometh to pass, when he heareth that I have lifted up my voice and call, that he leaveth his garment near me, and fleeth, and goeth without.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And she placeth his garment near her, until the coming in of his lord unto his house.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And she speaketh unto him according to these words, saying, 'The Hebrew servant whom thou hast brought unto us, hath come in unto me to play with me;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and it cometh to pass, when I lift my voice and call, that he leaveth his garment near me, and fleeth without.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it cometh to pass when his lord heareth the words of his wife, which she hath spoken unto him, saying, 'According to these things hath thy servant done to me,' that his anger burneth;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Joseph's lord taketh him, and putteth him unto the round-house, a place where the king's prisoners [are] bound; and he is there in the round-house.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah is with Joseph, and stretcheth out kindness unto him, and putteth his grace in the eyes of the chief of the round-house;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the chief of the round-house giveth into the hand of Joseph all the prisoners who [are] in the round-house, and of all that they are doing there, he hath been doer;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: the chief of the round-house seeth not anything under his hand, because Jehovah [is] with him, and that which he is doing Jehovah is causing to prosper.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it cometh to pass, after these things -- the butler of the king of Egypt and the baker have sinned against their lord, against the king of Egypt;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Pharaoh is wroth against his two eunuchs, against the chief of the butlers, and against the chief of the bakers,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and giveth them in charge in the house of the chief of the executioners, unto the round-house, the place where Joseph [is] a prisoner,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the chief of the executioners chargeth Joseph with them, and he serveth them; and they are days in charge.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they dream a dream both of them, each his dream in one night, each according to the interpretation of his dream, the butler and the baker whom the king of Egypt hath, who [are] prisoners in the round-house.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Joseph cometh in unto them in the morning, and seeth them, and lo, they [are] morose;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he asketh Pharaoh's eunuchs who [are] with him in charge in the house of his lord, saying, 'Wherefore [are] your faces sad to-day?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they say unto him, 'A dream we have dreamed, and there is no interpreter of it;' and Joseph saith unto them, 'Are not interpretations with God? recount, I pray you, to me.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the chief of the butlers recounteth his dream to Joseph, and saith to him, 'In my dream, then lo, a vine [is] before me!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and in the vine [are] three branches, and it [is] as it were flourishing; gone up hath its blossom, its clusters have ripened grapes;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Pharaoh's cup [is] in my hand, and I take the grapes and press them into the cup of Pharaoh, and I give the cup into the hand of Pharaoh.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Joseph saith to him, 'This [is] its interpretation: the three branches are three days;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: yet, within three days doth Pharaoh lift up thy head, and hath put thee back on thy station, and thou hast given the cup of Pharaoh into his hand, according to the former custom when thou wast his butler.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Surely if thou hast remembered me with thee, when it is well with thee, and hast done (I pray thee) kindness with me, and hast made mention of me unto Pharaoh, then hast thou brought me out from this house,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for I was really stolen from the land of the Hebrews; and here also have I done nothing that they have put me in the pit.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the chief of the bakers seeth that he hath interpreted good, and he saith unto Joseph, 'I also [am] in a dream, and lo, three baskets of white bread [are] on my head,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and in the uppermost basket [are] of all [kinds] of Pharaoh's food, work of a baker; and the birds are eating them out of the basket, from off my head.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Joseph answereth and saith, 'This [is] its interpretation: the three baskets are three days;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: yet, within three days doth Pharaoh lift up thy head from off thee, and hath hanged thee on a tree, and the birds have eaten thy flesh from off thee.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it cometh to pass, on the third day, Pharaoh's birthday, that he maketh a banquet to all his servants, and lifteth up the head of the chief of the butlers, and the head of the chief of the bakers among his servants,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he putteth back the chief of the butlers to his butlership, and he giveth the cup into the hand of Pharaoh;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the chief of the bakers he hath hanged, as Joseph hath interpreted to them;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the chief of the butlers hath not remembered Joseph, but forgetteth him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it cometh to pass, at the end of two years of days that Pharaoh is dreaming, and lo, he is standing by the River,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and lo, from the River coming up are seven kine, of fair appearance, and fat [in] flesh, and they feed among the reeds;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and lo, seven other kine are coming up after them out of the River, of bad appearance, and lean [in] flesh, and they stand near the kine on the edge of the River,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the kine of bad appearance and lean [in] flesh eat up the seven kine of fair appearance, and fat -- and Pharaoh awaketh.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he sleepeth, and dreameth a second time, and lo, seven ears are coming up on one stalk, fat and good,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and lo, seven ears, thin, and blasted with an east wind, are springing up after them;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the thin ears swallow the seven fat and full ears -- and Pharaoh awaketh, and lo, a dream.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it cometh to pass in the morning, that his spirit is moved, and he sendeth and calleth all the scribes of Egypt, and all its wise men, and Pharaoh recounteth to them his dream, and there is no interpreter of them to Pharaoh.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the chief of the butlers speaketh with Pharaoh, saying, 'My sin I mention this day:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Pharaoh hath been wroth against his servants, and giveth me into charge in the house of the chief of the executioners, me and the chief of the bakers;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and we dream a dream in one night, I and he, each according to the interpretation of his dream we have dreamed.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
