translations.text: And there [is] with us a youth, a Hebrew, servant to the chief of the executioners, and we recount to him, and he interpreteth to us our dreams, [to] each according to his dream hath he interpreted,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and it cometh to pass, as he hath interpreted to us so it hath been, me he put back on my station, and him he hanged.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Pharaoh sendeth and calleth Joseph, and they cause him to run out of the pit, and he shaveth, and changeth his garments, and cometh in unto Pharaoh.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Pharaoh saith unto Joseph, 'A dream I have dreamed, and there is no interpreter of it, and I -- I have heard concerning thee, saying, Thou understandest a dream to interpret it,'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Joseph answereth Pharaoh, saying, 'Without me -- God doth answer Pharaoh with peace.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Pharaoh speaketh unto Joseph: 'In my dream, lo, I am standing by the edge of the River,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and lo, out of the River coming up are seven kine, fat [in] flesh, and of fair form, and they feed among the reeds;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and lo, seven other kine are coming up after them, thin, and of very bad form, and lean [in] flesh; I have not seen like these in all the land of Egypt for badness.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And the lean and the bad kine eat up the first seven fat kine,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they come in unto their midst, and it hath not been known that they have come in unto their midst, and their appearance [is] bad as at the commencement; and I awake.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And I see in my dream, and lo, seven ears are coming up on one stalk, full and good;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and lo, seven ears, withered, thin, blasted with an east wind, are springing up after them;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the thin ears swallow the seven good ears; and I tell unto the scribes, and there is none declaring to me.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Joseph saith unto Pharaoh, 'The dream of Pharaoh is one: that which God is doing he hath declared to Pharaoh;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: the seven good kine are seven years, and the seven good ears are seven years, the dream is one;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the seven thin and bad kine which are coming up after them are seven years, and the seven empty ears, blasted with an east wind, are seven years of famine;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: this [is] the thing which I have spoken unto Pharaoh: That which God is doing, he hath shewn Pharaoh.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Lo, seven years are coming of great abundance in all the land of Egypt,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and seven years of famine have arisen after them, and all the plenty is forgotten in the land of Egypt, and the famine hath finished the land,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the plenty is not known in the land because of that famine afterwards, for it [is] very grievous.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And because of the repeating of the dream unto Pharaoh twice, surely the thing is established by God, and God is hastening to do it.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And now, let Pharaoh provide a man, intelligent and wise, and set him over the land of Egypt;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: let Pharaoh make and appoint overseers over the land, and receive a fifth of the land of Egypt in the seven years of plenty,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they gather all the food of these good years that are coming, and heap up corn under the hand of Pharaoh -- food in the cities; and they have kept [it],

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the food hath been for a store for the land, for the seven years of famine which are in the land of Egypt; and the land is cut off by the famine.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the thing is good in the eyes of Pharaoh, and in the eyes of all his servants,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Pharaoh saith unto his servants, 'Do we find like this, a man in whom the spirit of God [is]?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Pharaoh saith unto Joseph, 'After God's causing thee to know all this, there is none intelligent and wise as thou;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: thou -- thou art over my house, and at thy mouth do all my people kiss; only in the throne I am greater than thou.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Pharaoh saith unto Joseph, 'See, I have put thee over all the land of Egypt.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Pharaoh turneth aside his seal-ring from off his hand, and putteth it on the hand of Joseph, and clotheth him [with] garments of fine linen, and placeth a chain of gold on his neck,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and causeth him to ride in the second chariot which he hath, and they proclaim before him, 'Bow the knee!' and -- to put him over all the land of Egypt.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Pharaoh saith unto Joseph, 'I [am] Pharaoh, and without thee a man doth not lift up his hand and his foot in all the land of Egypt;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Pharaoh calleth Joseph's name Zaphnath-Paaneah, and he giveth to him Asenath daughter of Poti-Pherah, priest of On, for a wife, and Joseph goeth out over the land of Egypt.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Joseph [is] a son of thirty years in his standing before Pharaoh king of Egypt, and Joseph goeth out from the presence of Pharaoh, and passeth over through all the land of Egypt;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the land maketh in the seven years of plenty by handfuls.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he gathereth all the food of the seven years which have been in the land of Egypt, and putteth food in the cities; the food of the field which [is] round about [each] city hath he put in its midst;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Joseph gathereth corn as sand of the sea, multiplying exceedingly, until that he hath ceased to number, for there is no number.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And to Joseph were born two sons before the year of famine cometh, whom Asenath daughter of Poti-Pherah, priest of On, hath borne to him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Joseph calleth the name of the first-born Manasseh: 'for, God hath made me to forget all my labour, and all the house of my father;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the name of the second he hath called Ephraim: 'for, God hath caused me to be fruitful in the land of mine affliction.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the seven years of plenty are completed which have been in the land of Egypt,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the seven years of famine begin to come, as Joseph said, and famine is in all the lands, but in all the land of Egypt hath been bread;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and all the land of Egypt is famished, and the people crieth unto Pharaoh for bread, and Pharaoh saith to all the Egyptians, 'Go unto Joseph; that which he saith to you -- do.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the famine has been over all the face of the land, and Joseph openeth all [places] which have [corn] in them, and selleth to the Egyptians; and the famine is severe in the land of Egypt,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and all the earth hath come to Egypt, to buy, unto Joseph, for the famine was severe in all the earth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jacob seeth that there is corn in Egypt, and Jacob saith to his sons, 'Why do you look at each other?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: he saith also, 'Lo, I have heard that there is corn in Egypt, go down thither, and buy for us from thence, and we live and do not die;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the ten brethren of Joseph go down to buy corn in Egypt,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Benjamin, Joseph's brother, Jacob hath not sent with his brethren, for he said, 'Lest mischief meet him.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the sons of Israel come to buy in the midst of those coming, for the famine hath been in the land of Canaan,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Joseph is the ruler over the land, he who is selling to all the people of the land, and Joseph's brethren come and bow themselves to him -- face to the earth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Joseph seeth his brethren, and discerneth them, and maketh himself strange unto them, and speaketh with them sharp things, and saith unto them, 'From whence have ye come?' and they say, 'From the land of Canaan -- to buy food.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Joseph discerneth his brethren, but they have not discerned him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Joseph remembereth the dreams which he dreamed of them, and saith unto them, 'Ye [are] spies; to see the nakedness of the land ye have come.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they say unto him, 'No, my lord, but thy servants have come to buy food;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: we [are] all of us sons of one man, we [are] right men; thy servants have not been spies;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he saith unto them, 'No, but the nakedness of the land ye have come to see;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they say, 'Thy servants [are] twelve brethren; we [are] sons of one man in the land of Canaan, and lo, the young one [is] with our father to-day, and the one is not.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Joseph saith unto them, 'This [is] that which I have spoken unto you, saying, Ye [are] spies,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: by this ye are proved: Pharaoh liveth! if ye go out from this -- except by your young brother coming hither;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: send one of you, and let him bring your brother, and ye, remain ye bound, and let your words be proved, whether truth be with you: and if not -- Pharaoh liveth! surely ye [are] spies;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he removeth them unto charge three days.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Joseph saith unto them on the third day, 'This do and live; God I fear!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: if ye [are] right men, let one of your brethren be bound in the house of your ward, and ye, go, carry in corn [for] the famine of your houses,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and your young brother ye bring unto me, and your words are established, and ye die not;' and they do so.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they say one unto another, 'Verily we [are] guilty concerning our brother, because we saw the distress of his soul, in his making supplication unto us, and we did not hearken: therefore hath this distress come upon us.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Reuben answereth them, saying, 'Spake I not unto you, saying, Sin not against the lad? and ye hearkened not; and his blood also, lo, it is required.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they have not known that Joseph understandeth, for the interpreter [is] between them;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he turneth round from them, and weepeth, and turneth back unto them, and speaketh unto them, and taketh from them Simeon, and bindeth him before their eyes.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Joseph commandeth, and they fill their vessels [with] corn, also to put back the money of each unto his sack, and to give to them provision for the way; and one doth to them so.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they lift up their corn upon their asses, and go from thence,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the one openeth his sack to give provender to his ass at a lodging-place, and he seeth his money, and lo, it [is] in the mouth of his bag,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he saith unto his brethren, 'My money hath been put back, and also, lo, in my bag:' and their heart goeth out, and they tremble, one to another saying, 'What [is] this God hath done to us!'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they come in unto Jacob their father, to the land of Canaan, and they declare to him all the things meeting them, saying,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'The man, the lord of the land, hath spoken with us sharp things, and maketh us as spies of the land;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and we say unto him, We [are] right men, we have not been spies,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: we [are] twelve brethren, sons of our father, the one is not, and the young one [is] to-day with our father in the land of Canaan.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And the man, the lord of the land, saith unto us, By this I know that ye [are] right men -- one of your brethren leave with me, and [for] the famine of your houses take ye and go,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and bring your young brother unto me, and I know that ye [are] not spies, but ye [are] right men; your brother I give to you, and ye trade with the land.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it cometh to pass, they are emptying their sacks, and lo, the bundle of each man's silver [is] in his sack, and they see their bundles of silver, they and their father, and are afraid;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jacob their father saith unto them, 'Me ye have bereaved; Joseph is not, and Simeon is not, and Benjamin ye take -- against me have been all these.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Reuben speaketh unto his father, saying, 'My two sons thou dost put to death, if I bring him not in unto thee; give him into my hand, and I -- I bring him back unto thee;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he saith, 'My son doth not go down with you, for his brother [is] dead, and he by himself is left; when mischief hath met him in the way in which ye go, then ye have brought down my grey hairs in sorrow to sheol.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the famine [is] severe in the land;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and it cometh to pass, when they have finished eating the corn which they brought from Egypt, that their father saith unto them, 'Turn back, buy for us a little food.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Judah speaketh unto him, saying, 'The man protesting protested to us, saying, Ye do not see my face without your brother [being] with you;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: if thou art sending our brother with us, we go down, and buy for thee food,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and if thou art not sending -- we do not go down, for the man said unto us, Ye do not see my face without your brother [being] with you.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Israel saith, 'Why did ye evil to me, by declaring to the man that ye had yet a brother?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they say, 'The man asked diligently concerning us, and concerning our kindred, saying, Is your father yet alive? have ye a brother? and we declare to him according to the tenor of these things; do we certainly know that he will say, Bring down your brother?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Judah saith unto Israel his father, 'Send the youth with me, and we arise, and go, and live, and do not die, both we, and thou, and our infants.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I -- I am surety [for] him, from my hand thou dost require him; if I have not brought him in unto thee, and set him before thee -- then I have sinned against thee all the days;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for if we had not lingered, surely now we had returned these two times.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Israel their father saith unto them, 'If so, now, this do: take of the praised thing of the land in your vessels, and take down to the man a present, a little balm, and a little honey, spices and myrrh, nuts and almonds;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and double money take in your hand, even the money which is brought back in the mouth of your bags, ye take back in your hand, it may be it [is] an oversight.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And take your brother, and rise, turn back unto the man;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and God Almighty give to you mercies before the man, so that he hath sent to you your other brother and Benjamin; and I, when I am bereaved -- I am bereaved.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the men take this present, double money also they have taken in their hand, and Benjamin; and they rise, and go down to Egypt, and stand before Joseph;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Joseph seeth Benjamin with them, and saith to him who [is] over his house, 'Bring the men into the house, and slaughter an animal, and make ready, for with me do the men eat at noon.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the man doth as Joseph hath said, and the man bringeth in the men into the house of Joseph,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the men are afraid because they have been brought into the house of Joseph, and they say, 'For the matter of the money which was put back in our bags at the commencement are we brought in -- to roll himself upon us, and to throw himself on us, and to take us for servants -- our asses also.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they come nigh unto the man who [is] over the house of Joseph, and speak unto him at the opening of the house,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and say, 'O, my lord, we really come down at the commencement to buy food;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and it cometh to pass, when we have come in unto the lodging-place, and open our bags, that lo, each one's money [is] in the mouth of his bag, our money in its weight, and we bring it back in our hand;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and other money have we brought down in our hand to buy food; we have not known who put our money in our bags.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he saith, 'Peace to you, fear not: your God and the God of your father hath given to you hidden treasure in your bags, your money came unto me;' and he bringeth out Simeon unto them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the man bringeth in the men into Joseph's house, and giveth water, and they wash their feet; and he giveth provender for their asses,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they prepare the present until the coming of Joseph at noon, for they have heard that there they do eat bread.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Joseph cometh into the house, and they bring to him the present which [is] in their hand, into the house, and bow themselves to him, to the earth;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he asketh of them of peace, and saith, 'Is your father well? the aged man of whom ye have spoken, is he yet alive?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they say, 'Thy servant our father [is] well, he is yet alive;' and they bow, and do obeisance.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he lifteth up his eyes, and seeth Benjamin his brother, his mother's son, and saith, 'Is this your young brother, of whom ye have spoken unto me?' and he saith, 'God favour thee, my son.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Joseph hasteth, for his bowels have been moved for his brother, and he seeketh to weep, and entereth the inner chamber, and weepeth there;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he washeth his face, and goeth out, and refraineth himself, and saith, 'Place bread.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they place for him by himself, and for them by themselves, and for the Egyptians who are eating with him by themselves: for the Egyptians are unable to eat bread with the Hebrews, for it [is] an abomination to the Egyptians.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they sit before him, the first-born according to his birthright, and the young one according to his youth, and the men wonder one at another;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he lifteth up gifts from before him unto them, and the gift of Benjamin is five hands more than the gifts of all of them; and they drink, yea, they drink abundantly with him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he commandeth him who [is] over his house, saying, 'Fill the bags of the men [with] food, as they are able to bear, and put the money of each in the mouth of his bag;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and my cup, the silver cup, thou dost put in the mouth of the bag of the young one, and his corn-money;' and he doth according to the word of Joseph which he hath spoken.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The morning is bright, and the men have been sent away, they and their asses --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: they have gone out of the city -- they have not gone far off -- and Joseph hath said to him who [is] over his house, 'Rise, pursue after the men; and thou hast overtaken them, and thou hast said unto them, Why have ye recompensed evil for good?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Is not this that with which my lord drinketh? and he observeth diligently with it; ye have done evil [in] that which ye have done.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he overtaketh them, and speaketh unto them these words,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they say unto him, 'Why doth my lord speak according to these words? far be it from thy servants to do according to this word;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: lo, the money which we found in the mouth of our bags we brought back unto thee from the land of Canaan, and how do we steal from the house of thy lord silver or gold?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: with whomsoever of thy servants it is found, he hath died, and we also are to my lord for servants.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he saith, 'Now, also, according to your words, so it [is]; he with whom it is found becometh my servant, and ye are acquitted;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they hasten and take down each his bag to the earth, and each openeth his bag;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he searcheth -- at the eldest he hath begun, and at the youngest he hath completed -- and the cup is found in the bag of Benjamin;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they rend their garments, and each ladeth his ass, and they turn back to the city.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Judah -- his brethren also -- cometh in unto the house of Joseph, and he is yet there, and they fall before him to the earth;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Joseph saith to them, 'What [is] this deed that ye have done? have ye not known that a man like me doth diligently observe?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Judah saith, 'What do we say to my lord? what do we speak? and what -- do we justify ourselves? God hath found out the iniquity of thy servants; lo, we [are] servants to my lord, both we, and he in whose hand the cup hath been found;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he saith, 'Far be it from me to do this; the man in whose hand the cup hath been found, he becometh my servant; and ye, go ye up in peace unto your father.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Judah cometh nigh unto him, and saith, 'O, my lord, let thy servant speak, I pray thee, a word in the ears of my lord, and let not thine anger burn against thy servant -- for thou art as Pharaoh.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: My lord hath asked his servants, saying, Have ye a father or brother?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and we say unto my lord, We have a father, an aged one, and a child of old age, a little one; and his brother died, and he is left alone of his mother, and his father hath loved him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And thou sayest unto thy servants, Bring him down unto me, and I set mine eye upon him;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and we say unto my lord, The youth is not able to leave his father, when he hath left his father, then he hath died;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and thou sayest unto thy servants, If your young brother come not down with you, ye add not to see my face.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And it cometh to pass, that we have come up unto thy servant my father, that we declare to him the words of my lord;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and our father saith, Turn back, buy for us a little food,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and we say, We are not able to go down; if our young brother is with us, then we have gone down; for we are not able to see the man's face, and our young brother not with us.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And thy servant my father saith unto us, Ye -- ye have known that two did my wife bare to me,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the one goeth out from me, and I say, Surely he is torn -- torn! and I have not seen him since;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: when ye have taken also this from my presence, and mischief hath met him, then ye have brought down my grey hairs with evil to sheol.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And now, at my coming in unto thy servant my father, and the youth not with us (and his soul is bound up in his soul),

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: then it hath come to pass when he seeth that the youth is not, that he hath died, and thy servants have brought down the grey hairs of thy servant our father with sorrow to sheol;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for thy servant obtained the youth by surety from my father, saying, If I bring him not in unto thee -- then I have sinned against my father all the days.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And now, let thy servant, I pray thee, abide instead of the youth a servant to my lord, and the youth goeth up with his brethren,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for how do I go up unto my father, and the youth not with me? lest I look on the evil which doth find my father.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Joseph hath not been able to refrain himself before all those standing by him, and he calleth, 'Put out every man from me;' and no man hath stood with him when Joseph maketh himself known unto his brethren,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he giveth forth his voice in weeping, and the Egyptians hear, and the house of Pharaoh heareth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Joseph saith unto his brethren, 'I [am] Joseph, is my father yet alive?' and his brethren have not been able to answer him, for they have been troubled at his presence.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Joseph saith unto his brethren, 'Come nigh unto me, I pray you,' and they come nigh; and he saith, 'I [am] Joseph, your brother, whom ye sold into Egypt;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and now, be not grieved, nor let it be displeasing in your eyes that ye sold me hither, for to preserve life hath God sent me before you.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Because these two years the famine [is] in the heart of the land, and yet [are] five years, [in] which there is neither ploughing nor harvest;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and God sendeth me before you, to place of you a remnant in the land, and to give life to you by a great escape;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and now, ye -- ye have not sent me hither, but God, and He doth set me for a father to Pharaoh, and for lord to all his house, and ruler over all the land of Egypt.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Haste, and go up unto my father, then ye have said to him, Thus said Joseph thy son, God hath set me for lord to all Egypt; come down unto me, stay not,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and thou hast dwelt in the land of Goshen, and been near unto me, thou and thy sons, and thy son's sons, and thy flock, and thy herd, and all that thou hast,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and I have nourished thee there -- for yet [are] five years of famine -- lest thou become poor, thou and thy household, and all that thou hast.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And lo, your eyes are seeing, and the eyes of my brother Benjamin, that [it is] my mouth which is speaking unto you;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and ye have declared to my father all my honour in Egypt, and all that ye have seen, and ye have hasted, and have brought down my father hither.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he falleth on the neck of Benjamin his brother, and weepeth, and Benjamin hath wept on his neck;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he kisseth all his brethren, and weepeth over them; and afterwards have his brethren spoken with him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the sound hath been heard in the house of Pharaoh, saying, 'Come have the brethren of Joseph;' and it is good in the eyes of Pharaoh, and in the eyes of his servants,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Pharaoh saith unto Joseph, 'Say unto thy brethren, This do ye: lade your beasts, and go, enter ye the land of Canaan,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and take your father, and your households, and come unto me, and I give to you the good of the land of Egypt, and eat ye the fat of the land.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Yea, thou -- thou hast been commanded: this do ye, take for yourselves out of the land of Egypt, waggons for your infants, and for your wives, and ye have brought your father, and come;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and your eye hath no pity on your vessels, for the good of all the land of Egypt [is] yours.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the sons of Israel do so, and Joseph giveth waggons to them by the command of Pharaoh, and he giveth to them provision for the way;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: to all of them hath he given -- to each changes of garments, and to Benjamin he hath given three hundred silverlings, and five changes of garments;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and to his father he hath sent thus: ten asses bearing of the good things of Egypt, and ten she-asses bearing corn and bread, even food for his father for the way.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he sendeth his brethren away, and they go; and he saith unto them, 'Be not angry in the way.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they go up out of Egypt, and come in to the land of Canaan, unto Jacob their father,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they declare to him, saying, 'Joseph [is] yet alive,' and that he [is] ruler over all the land of Egypt; and his heart ceaseth, for he hath not given credence to them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they speak unto him all the words of Joseph, which he hath spoken unto them, and he seeth the waggons which Joseph hath sent to bear him away, and live doth the spirit of Jacob their father;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Israel saith, 'Enough! Joseph my son [is] yet alive; I go and see him before I die.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Israel journeyeth, and all that he hath, and cometh in to Beer-Sheba, and sacrificeth sacrifices to the God of his father Isaac;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and God speaketh to Israel in visions of the night, and saith, 'Jacob, Jacob;' and he saith, 'Here [am] I.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And He saith, 'I [am] God, God of thy father, be not afraid of going down to Egypt, for for a great nation I set thee there;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I -- I go down with thee to Egypt, and I -- I also certainly bring thee up, and Joseph doth put his hand on thine eyes.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jacob riseth from Beer-Sheba, and the sons of Israel bear away Jacob their father, And their infants, and their wives, in the waggons which Pharaoh hath sent to bear him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they take their cattle, and their goods which they have acquired in the land of Canaan, and come into Egypt -- Jacob, and all his seed with him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: his sons, and his sons' sons with him, his daughters, and his sons' daughters, yea, all his seed he brought with him into Egypt.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And these [are] the names of the sons of Israel who are coming into Egypt: Jacob and his sons, Jacob's first-born, Reuben.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And sons of Reuben: Hanoch, and Phallu, and Hezron, and Carmi.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And sons of Simeon: Jemuel, and Jamin, and Ohad, and Jachin, and Zohar, and Shaul son of the Canaanitess.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And sons of Levi: Gershon, Kohath, and Merari.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And sons of Judah: Er, and Onan, and Shelah, and Pharez, and Zarah, (and Er and Onan die in the land of Canaan.) And sons of Pharez are Hezron and Hamul.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And sons of Issachar: Tola, and Phuvah, and Job, and Shimron.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And sons of Zebulun: Sered, and Elon, and Jahleel.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: These [are] sons of Leah whom she bare to Jacob in Padan-Aram, and Dinah his daughter; all the persons of his sons and his daughters [are] thirty and three.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And sons of Gad: Ziphion, and Haggi, Shuni, and Ezbon, Eri, and Arodi, and Areli.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And sons of Asher: Jimnah, and Ishuah, and Isui, and Beriah, and Serah their sister. And sons of Beriah: Heber and Malchiel.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: These [are] sons of Zilpah, whom Laban gave to Leah his daughter, and she beareth these to Jacob -- sixteen persons.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Sons of Rachel, Jacob's wife: Joseph and Benjamin.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And born to Joseph in the land of Egypt (whom Asenath daughter of Poti-Pherah, priest of On, hath borne to him) [are] Manasseh and Ephraim.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And sons of Benjamin: Belah, and Becher, and Ashbel, Gera, and Naaman, Ehi, and Rosh, Muppim, and Huppim, and Ard.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: These [are] sons of Rachel, who were born to Jacob; all the persons [are] fourteen.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And sons of Dan: Hushim.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And sons of Naphtali: Jahzeel, and Guni, and Jezer, and Shillem.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: These [are] sons of Bilhah, whom Laban gave to Rachel his daughter; and she beareth these to Jacob -- all the persons [are] seven.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: All the persons who are coming to Jacob to Egypt, coming out of his thigh, apart from the wives of Jacob's sons, all the persons [are] sixty and six.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the sons of Joseph who have been born to him in Egypt [are] two persons. All the persons of the house of Jacob who are coming into Egypt [are] seventy.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Judah he hath sent before him unto Joseph, to direct before him to Goshen, and they come into the land of Goshen;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Joseph harnesseth his chariot, and goeth up to meet Israel his father, to Goshen, and appeareth unto him, and falleth on his neck, and weepeth on his neck again;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Israel saith unto Joseph, 'Let me die this time, after my seeing thy face, for thou [art] yet alive.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Joseph saith unto his brethren, and unto the house of his father, 'I go up, and declare to Pharaoh, and say unto him, My brethren, and the house of my father who [are] in the land of Canaan have come in unto me;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the men [are] feeders of a flock, for they have been men of cattle; and their flock, and their herd, and all that they have, they have brought.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And it hath come to pass when Pharaoh calleth for you, and hath said, What [are] your works?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: that ye have said, Thy servants have been men of cattle from our youth, even until now, both we and our fathers, -- in order that ye may dwell in the land of Goshen, for the abomination of the Egyptians is every one feeding a flock.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Joseph cometh, and declareth to Pharaoh, and saith, 'My father, and my brethren, and their flock, and their herd, and all they have, have come from the land of Canaan, and lo, they [are] in the land of Goshen.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And out of his brethren he hath taken five men, and setteth them before Pharaoh;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Pharaoh saith unto his brethren, 'What [are] your works?' and they say unto Pharaoh, 'Thy servants [are] feeders of a flock, both we and our fathers;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they say unto Pharaoh, 'To sojourn in the land we have come, for there is no pasture for the flock which thy servants have, for grievous [is] the famine in the land of Canaan; and now, let thy servants, we pray thee, dwell in the land of Goshen.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Pharaoh speaketh unto Joseph, saying, 'Thy father and thy brethren have come unto thee:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: the land of Egypt is before thee; in the best of the land cause thy father and thy brethren to dwell -- they dwell in the land of Goshen, and if thou hast known, and there are among them men of ability, then thou hast set them heads over the cattle I have.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Joseph bringeth in Jacob his father, and causeth him to stand before Pharaoh; and Jacob blesseth Pharaoh.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Pharaoh saith unto Jacob, 'How many [are] the days of the years of thy life?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jacob saith unto Pharaoh, 'The days of the years of my sojournings [are] an hundred and thirty years; few and evil have been the days of the years of my life, and they have not reached the days of the years of the life of my fathers, in the days of their sojournings.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jacob blesseth Pharaoh, and goeth out from before Pharaoh.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Joseph settleth his father and his brethren, and giveth to them a possession in the land of Egypt, in the best of the land, in the land of Rameses, as Pharaoh commanded;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Joseph nourisheth his father, and his brethren, and all the house of his father [with] bread, according to the mouth of the infants.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And there is no bread in all the land, for the famine [is] very grievous, and the land of Egypt and the land of Canaan are feeble because of the famine;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Joseph gathereth all the silver that is found in the land of Egypt, and in the land of Canaan, for the corn that they are buying, and Joseph bringeth the silver into the house of Pharaoh.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the silver is consumed out of the land of Egypt, and out of the land of Canaan, and all the Egyptians come in unto Joseph, saying, 'Give to us bread -- why do we die before thee, though the money hath ceased?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Joseph saith, 'Give your cattle; and I give to you for your cattle, if the money hath ceased.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they bring in their cattle unto Joseph, and Joseph giveth to them bread, for the horses, and for the cattle of the flock, and for the cattle of the herd, and for the asses; and he tendeth them with bread, for all their cattle, during that year.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And that year is finished, and they come in unto him on the second year, and say to him, 'We do not hide from my lord, that since the money hath been finished, and possession of the cattle [is] unto my lord, there hath not been left before my lord save our bodies, and our ground;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: why do we die before thine eyes, both we and our ground? buy us and our ground for bread, and we and our ground are servants to Pharaoh; and give seed, and we live, and die not, and the ground is not desolate.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Joseph buyeth all the ground of Egypt for Pharaoh, for the Egyptians have sold each his field, for the famine hath been severe upon them, and the land becometh Pharaoh's;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: as to the people he hath removed them to cities from the [one] end of the border of Egypt even unto its [other] end.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Only the ground of the priests he hath not bought, for the priests have a portion from Pharaoh, and they have eaten their portion which Pharaoh hath given to them, therefore they have not sold their ground.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Joseph saith unto the people, 'Lo, I have bought you to-day and your ground for Pharaoh; lo, seed for you, and ye have sown the ground,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and it hath come to pass in the increases, that ye have given a fifth to Pharaoh, and four of the parts are for yourselves, for seed of the field, and for your food, and for those who [are] in your houses, and for food for your infants.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they say, 'Thou hast revived us; we find grace in the eyes of my lord, and have been servants to Pharaoh;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Joseph setteth it for a statute unto this day, concerning the ground of Egypt, [that] Pharaoh hath a fifth; only the ground of the priests alone hath not become Pharaoh's.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Israel dwelleth in the land of Egypt, in the land of Goshen, and they have possession in it, and are fruitful, and multiply exceedingly;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jacob liveth in the land of Egypt seventeen years, and the days of Jacob, the years of his life, are an hundred and forty and seven years.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the days of Israel are near to die, and he calleth for his son, for Joseph, and saith to him, 'If, I pray thee, I have found grace in thine eyes, put, I pray thee, thy hand under my thigh, and thou hast done with me kindness and truth; bury me not, I pray thee, in Egypt,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and I have lain with my fathers, and thou hast borne me out of Egypt, and buried me in their burying-place.' And he saith, 'I -- I do according to thy word;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he saith, 'Swear to me;' and he sweareth to him, and Israel boweth himself on the head of the bed.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it cometh to pass, after these things, that [one] saith to Joseph, 'Lo, thy father is sick;' and he taketh his two sons with him, Manasseh and Ephraim.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And [one] declareth to Jacob, and saith, 'Lo, thy son Joseph is coming unto thee;' and Israel doth strengthen himself, and sit upon the bed.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jacob saith unto Joseph, 'God Almighty hath appeared unto me, in Luz, in the land of Canaan, and blesseth me,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and saith unto me, Lo, I am making thee fruitful, and have multiplied thee, and given thee for an assembly of peoples, and given this land to thy seed after thee, a possession age-during.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And now, thy two sons, who are born to thee in the land of Egypt, before my coming unto thee to Egypt, mine they [are]; Ephraim and Manasseh, as Reuben and Simeon they are mine;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and thy family which thou hast begotten after them are thine; by the name of their brethren they are called in their inheritance.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And I -- in my coming in from Padan-Aram Rachel hath died by me in the land of Canaan, in the way, while yet a kibrath of land to enter Ephrata, and I bury her there in the way of Ephrata, which [is] Bethlehem.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Israel seeth the sons of Joseph, and saith, 'Who [are] these?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Joseph saith unto his father, 'They [are] my sons, whom God hath given to me in this [place];' and he saith, 'Bring them, I pray thee, unto me, and I bless them.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the eyes of Israel have been heavy from age, he is unable to see; and he bringeth them nigh unto him, and he kisseth them, and cleaveth to them;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Israel saith unto Joseph, 'To see thy face I had not thought, and lo, God hath shewed me also thy seed.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Joseph bringeth them out from between his knees, and boweth himself on his face to the earth;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Joseph taketh them both, Ephraim in his right hand towards Israel's left, and Manasseh in his left towards Israel's right, and bringeth [them] nigh to him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Israel putteth out his right hand, and placeth [it] upon the head of Ephraim, who [is] the younger, and his left hand upon the head of Manasseh; he hath guided his hands wisely, for Manasseh [is] the first-born.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he blesseth Joseph, and saith, 'God, before whom my fathers Abraham and Isaac walked habitually: God who is feeding me from my being unto this day:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: the Messenger who is redeeming me from all evil doth bless the youths, and my name is called upon them, and the name of my fathers Abraham and Isaac; and they increase into a multitude in the midst of the land.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Joseph seeth that his father setteth his right hand on the head of Ephraim, and it is wrong in his eyes, and he supporteth the hand of his father to turn it aside from off the head of Ephraim to the head of Manasseh;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Joseph saith unto his father, 'Not so, my father, for this [is] the first-born; set thy right hand on his head.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And his father refuseth, and saith, 'I have known, my son, I have known; he also becometh a people, and he also is great, and yet, his young brother is greater than he, and his seed is the fulness of the nations;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he blesseth them in that day, saying, 'By thee doth Israel bless, saying, God set thee as Ephraim and as Manasseh;' and he setteth Ephraim before Manasseh.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Israel saith unto Joseph, 'Lo, I am dying, and God hath been with you, and hath brought you back unto the land of your fathers;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and I -- I have given to thee one portion above thy brethren, which I have taken out of the hand of the Amorite by my sword and by my bow.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jacob calleth unto his sons and saith, 'Be gathered together, and I declare to you that which doth happen with you in the latter end of the days.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Be assembled, and hear, sons of Jacob, And hearken unto Israel your father.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Reuben! my first-born thou, My power, and beginning of my strength, The abundance of exaltation, And the abundance of strength;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Unstable as water, thou art not abundant; For thou hast gone up thy father's bed; Then thou hast polluted: My couch he went up!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Simeon and Levi [are] brethren! Instruments of violence -- their espousals!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Into their secret, come not, O my soul! Unto their assembly be not united, O mine honour; For in their anger they slew a man, And in their self-will eradicated a prince.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Cursed [is] their anger, for [it is] fierce, And their wrath, for [it is] sharp; I divide them in Jacob, And I scatter them in Israel.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Judah! thou -- thy brethren praise thee! Thy hand [is] on the neck of thine enemies, Sons of thy father bow themselves to thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: A lion's whelp [is] Judah, For prey, my son, thou hast gone up; He hath bent, he hath crouched as a lion, And as a lioness; who causeth him to arise?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The sceptre turneth not aside from Judah, And a lawgiver from between his feet, Till his Seed come; And his [is] the obedience of peoples.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Binding to the vine his ass, And to the choice vine the colt of his ass, He hath washed in wine his clothing, And in the blood of grapes his covering;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Red [are] eyes with wine, And white [are] teeth with milk!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Zebulun at a haven of the seas doth dwell, And he [is] for a haven of ships; And his side [is] unto Zidon.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Issacher [is] a strong ass, Crouching between the two folds;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he seeth rest that [it is] good, And the land that [it is] pleasant, And he inclineth his shoulder to bear, And is to tribute a servant.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Dan doth judge his people, As one of the tribes of Israel;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Dan is a serpent by the way, An adder by the path, Which is biting the horse's heels, And its rider falleth backward.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For Thy salvation I have waited, Jehovah!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Gad! a troop assaulteth him, But he assaulteth last.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Out of Asher his bread [is] fat; And he giveth dainties of a king.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Naphtali [is] a hind sent away, Who is giving beauteous young ones.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Joseph [is] a fruitful son; A fruitful son by a fountain, Daughters step over the wall;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And embitter him -- yea, they have striven, Yea, hate him do archers;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And his bow abideth in strength, And strengthened are the arms of his hands By the hands of the Mighty One of Jacob, Whence is a shepherd, a son of Israel.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: By the God of thy father who helpeth thee, And the Mighty One who blesseth thee, Blessings of the heavens from above, Blessings of the deep lying under, Blessings of breasts and womb; --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thy father's blessings have been mighty Above the blessings of my progenitors, Unto the limit of the heights age-during They are for the head of Joseph, And for the crown of the one Separate [from] his brethren.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Benjamin! a wolf teareth; In the morning he eateth prey, And at evening he apportioneth spoil.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: All these [are] the twelve tribes of Israel, and this [is] that which their father hath spoken unto them, and he blesseth them; each according to his blessing he hath blessed them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he commandeth them, and saith unto them, 'I am being gathered unto my people; bury me by my fathers, at the cave which [is] in the field of Ephron the Hittite;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: in the cave which [is] in the field of Machpelah, which [is] on the front of Mamre, in the land of Canaan, which Abraham bought with the field from Ephron the Hittite for a possession of a burying-place;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: (there they buried Abraham and Sarah his wife; there they buried Isaac and Rebekah his wife; and there I buried Leah);

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: the purchase of the field and of the cave which [is] in it, [is] from Sons of Heth.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jacob finisheth commanding his sons, and gathereth up his feet unto the bed, and expireth, and is gathered unto his people.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Joseph falleth on his father's face, and weepeth over him, and kisseth him;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Joseph commandeth his servants, the physicians, to embalm his father, and the physicians embalm Israel;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they fulfil for him forty days, for so they fulfil the days of the embalmed, and the Egyptians weep for him seventy days.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the days of his weeping pass away, and Joseph speaketh unto the house of Pharaoh, saying, 'If, I pray you, I have found grace in your eyes, speak, I pray you, in the ears of Pharaoh, saying,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: My father caused me to swear, saying, Lo, I am dying; in my burying-place which I have prepared for myself in the land of Canaan, there dost thou bury me; and now, let me go up, I pray thee, and bury my father, and return;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Pharaoh saith, 'Go up and bury thy father, as he caused thee to swear.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Joseph goeth up to bury his father, and go up with him do all the servants of Pharaoh, elders of his house, and all the elders of the land of Egypt,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and all the house of Joseph, and his brethren, and the house of his father; only their infants, and their flock, and their herd, have they left in the land of Goshen;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and there go up with him both chariot and horsemen, and the camp is very great.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they come unto the threshing-floor of Atad, which [is] beyond the Jordan, and they lament there, a lamentation great and very grievous; and he maketh for his father a mourning seven days,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the inhabitant of the land, the Canaanite, see the mourning in the threshing-floor of Atad, and say, 'A grievous mourning [is] this to the Egyptians;' therefore hath [one] called its name 'The mourning of the Egyptians,' which [is] beyond the Jordan.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And his sons do to him so as he commanded them,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and his sons bear him away to the land of Canaan, and bury him in the cave of the field of Machpelah, which Abraham bought with the field for a possession of a burying-place, from Ephron the Hittite, on the front of Mamre.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Joseph turneth back to Egypt, he and his brethren, and all who are going up with him to bury his father, after his burying his father.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the brethren of Joseph see that their father is dead, and say, 'Peradventure Joseph doth hate us, and doth certainly return to us all the evil which we did with him.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they give a charge for Joseph, saying, 'Thy father commanded before his death, saying,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thus ye do say to Joseph, I pray thee, bear, I pray thee, with the transgression of thy brethren, and their sin, for they have done thee evil; and now, bear, we pray thee, with the transgression of the servants of the God of thy father;' and Joseph weepeth in their speaking unto him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And his brethren also go and fall before him, and say, 'Lo, we [are] to thee for servants.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Joseph saith unto them, 'Fear not, for [am] I in the place of God?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: As for you, ye devised against me evil -- God devised it for good, in order to do as [at] this day, to keep alive a numerous people;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and now, fear not: I do nourish you and your infants;' and he comforteth them, and speaketh unto their heart.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Joseph dwelleth in Egypt, he and the house of his father, and Joseph liveth a hundred and ten years,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Joseph looketh on Ephraim's sons of the third [generation]; sons also of Machir, son of Manasseh, have been born on the knees of Joseph.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Joseph saith unto his brethren, 'I am dying, and God doth certainly inspect you, and hath caused you to go up from this land, unto the land which He hath sworn to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Joseph causeth the sons of Israel to swear, saying, 'God doth certainly inspect you, and ye have brought up my bones from this [place].'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Joseph dieth, a son of an hundred and ten years, and they embalm him, and he is put into a coffin in Egypt.
